title: Copilot Chat tools picker sometimes switches from global tool selection to session-local minimal tools
body:
Type: Bug

## Summary

In VS Code GitHub Copilot Chat, while using the built-in generic Agent mode without custom agents or prompt files, the tools picker sometimes switches from my global tool selection to a session-local minimal selection.

I normally have around 100 tools enabled globally for the default Agent. The tools picker shows:

> Selected tools are applied globally to all chat sessions using the default agent.

However, after some time, a specific chat session can show only 3 selected tools, with a different message:

> Selected tools were configured only for this chat session.

At that point, built-in tools such as edit / execute / read / search / web, extension tools, and MCP tools are unchecked for that chat session. Manually re-selecting tools fixes the session temporarily, but the issue can reappear.

This is not specific to MCP tools. MCP is just one visible symptom because MCP tools become unavailable along with built-in and extension tools.

## Why this seems different from existing issues

I found related issues, but they do not seem to describe this exact behavior:

- #266058: tool enablement was per-window / not global; fixed by storing global tools selection per profile.
- #269431: MCP/tools were re-selected unexpectedly; closed as duplicate of #266058.
- #272320: per-workspace tool configuration / selections reset between sessions; closed as duplicate of #266058.
- #275628: opening the tools list too early during startup can result in every tool being selected. This is related to tools picker state timing, but the behavior here is the opposite: a specific session is reduced to a minimal tool set.

In my case, the global selection still appears to be intact, but the current chat session has a session-local override with only a few tools enabled.

## Environment

- VS Code: 1.122.1
- VS Code commit: 8761a5560cfd65fdd19ce7e2bd18dab5c0a4d84e
- Architecture: x64
- GitHub Copilot Chat extension: 0.50.1
- OS: Windows 11
- Mode: built-in generic Agent mode
- Not using a custom agent for this chat
- Not invoking a prompt file with a `tools` frontmatter field
- Number of globally selected tools shown in the picker: 106
- Minimal session-local state shown in the picker: 3 selected tools

## Observed UI states

Working/global state:

- Tools picker shows 106 selected tools.
- Message says selected tools are applied globally to all chat sessions using the default agent.
- Built-in groups such as agent, edit, execute, read, search, todo, vscode, web, plus extension/MCP groups, are checked.

Broken/session-local state:

- Tools picker shows 3 selected tools.
- Message says selected tools were configured only for this chat session.
- `agent` remains checked, but edit / execute / read / search / todo / vscode / web / extension/MCP groups are unchecked.
- The agent then cannot use tools that were globally enabled.

## Local state observation

I inspected VS Code global storage and found `chat/selectedTools` in `User/globalStorage/state.vscdb`.

At the time I inspected it, the global state was not minimal:

```text
toolSetEntries total 18 / true 15 / false 3
toolEntries total 142 / true 106 / false 36
```

So the global/default tool selection appears to remain broad. The issue looks like a session-local selected-tools override is being created, restored, or applied unexpectedly for an ordinary Agent chat session.

## Expected behavior

When using the built-in generic Agent mode without custom agents or prompt files, the tools picker should continue to use the global default Agent tool selection unless I explicitly configure tools for the current chat session.

A chat session should not silently switch to a minimal session-local tools selection.

## Actual behavior

A chat session sometimes ends up with a session-local tools selection of only 3 tools. This disables built-in tools, extension tools, and MCP tools for that session, despite the global Agent tool selection being configured with around 100 tools.

## Impact

This breaks Agent workflows unexpectedly. The agent may suddenly be unable to read files, edit files, run commands, search the workspace, use web tools, or call MCP tools. It is also hard to notice until a tool-dependent request fails.

## Workaround

Open the tools picker and manually re-select the desired tools. This works temporarily for the affected session.

## Additional notes

I also have this setting enabled:

```jsonc
"chat.alternativeToolAction.enabled": true
```

I am not sure whether this is related, but the bug appears to involve a mismatch between global tool selection and session-local tool selection.

I can provide screenshots showing the two tools picker states:

1. 106 selected tools with the global-default message.
2. 3 selected tools with the current-chat-session-only message.

title: Mes crédits AI utilisé augmente tout seul ! sans aucun prompt supplémentaire
body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version:
- VS Code Version:
- OS Version:
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode):
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet):
- Logs:

Steps to Reproduce:

Depuis hier, mon VS Code m'affiche 7 000 crédits IA.
Je n'avais même pas fait un seul prompt que déjà 54 crédits étaient utilisés…
Je fais un prompt, ça monte à 104 crédits… Je n'ai plus rien fait comme prompt pendant 30 minutes, le temps de chercher quelques informations. Je reviens sur VS Code… je suis passé à 149 crédits utilisés !!! Alors qu'il n'y avait eu aucun prompt supplémentaire… 

C'est quoi ce truc ?! On se moque de nous !
Il faut qu'on m'explique comment des crédits diminuent sans aucun prompt !

title: benchmark: comparar consumo de tokens entre CLI e Code7 (agent host)
body:
## Objetivo

Comparar o custo real de tokens por turno entre o uso direto do `claude` CLI e o uso via Code7 (agent host / claude-agent-sdk), rodando as mesmas prompts nos dois ambientes.

## Motivação

O Code7 envia um system prompt maior (plugins, skills, agent customizations) que pode encarecer o custo por sessão. Com prompt caching, o impacto se dilui após o primeiro turno — mas precisa ser medido para confirmar se vale mudar o modelo padrão de `claude-opus-4-8` para `claude-sonnet-4-6`.

## Como coletar os dados

### Code7
- Habilitar `chat.agentHost.ipcLoggingEnabled: true`
- Cada turno emite um evento `session/usage` nos arquivos `.jsonl` em `~/.config/VS Code7/logs/*/ahp/`
- Campos: `inputTokens`, `cacheReadTokens`, `outputTokens`, `model`

### CLI
- Rodar prompts via `claude --print "<prompt>"` (modo não-interativo)
- Capturar uso via `--output-format json` ou via logs do `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` isolado

## Plano de implementação sugerido

1. Definir um conjunto fixo de N prompts (ex: 10) de complexidade variada
2. Rodar as N prompts no CLI (`claude --print`) e capturar token usage
3. Rodar as mesmas N prompts no Code7 via IPC e capturar `session/usage`
4. Medir pelo menos 5–10 turnos por ambiente para capturar o comportamento pós-cache (não só o cold start)
5. Calcular custo estimado por turno com os preços atuais de cada modelo
6. Gerar relatório comparativo: CLI vs Code7, cold start vs cache aquecido

## Critérios de sucesso

- [ ] Script automatizado que roda as prompts nos dois ambientes
- [ ] Relatório com custo médio por turno (pós-cache) por ambiente
- [ ] Conclusão sobre se vale mudar o modelo padrão para Sonnet 4.6

title: CoPilot Chat
body:
Type: Performance Issue I had my Copilot credits reset today and I was excited to get back to work. My first task was to fix a regression bug that my Copilot agent introduced. I started a fresh chat (As recommended online). I asked the agent to implement a simple fix. 5 minutes later I had use 25% of my monthly credits. I had selected the so-called "10% cheaper" auto AI agent. After a lonnnnggg profanity laced scream, I kept working and the token usage didn't improve. In less than 1 hour, I used 36% of my monthly AI tokens. A token policy that gave me 40 hours / month of AI agent now gives me <4 hours. This is not an acceptable cost for what is delivered. I am freelance and I am not going to spend insane money so I can clumsily create sub-par apps with AI agents that constantly break my code, regardless of how many requirements and regression tests I define. (I call it regressive AI) If this is the new normal, please return my money. It is time for me to retire. title: Terminal commands fail when Windows sandboxing is enabled
body:
Testing #319218

On Windows, enabled the sandbox setting after starting a session.

Any command (even a simple `echo`) fails with:

```
Error: failure - <exited with error: Failed to load native module: conpty.node, checked: build/Release, build/Debug, prebuilds/win32-x64: Error: Cannot find module './prebuilds/win32-x64/conpty.node'
Require stack:
- c:\Users\kycutler\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code Insiders\1ec0e88824\resources\app\extensions\copilot\node_modules\@github\copilot\sdk\index.js>
```

title: Copilot CLI can still read files outside the workspace with sandboxing enabled on Windows
body: Testing #319218 On Windows -- started a session, then enabled the `chat.agent.sandbox.enabledWindows` flag. 🐛 The agent can still see the file outside of the workspace using the `read` tool. Top request has sandboxing disabled, bottom request has it enabled: Opened https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319384 for the terminal issue

title: Copilot is consuming 51% of monthly budget with just 2 simple prompts...
body: This after one prompt asking to debug (&plan to fix) a communication issue, and a short prompt asking to implement the planned fix. And this is not even a large codebase, this is a relatively simple embedded project with just 10k SLOC (and of that only 2% were relevant to the given query!)! How is anyone seriously supposed to use Github Copilot if essentially one prompt consumes more than 50% of a month's budget?? Copilot WAS once great and delivered better prompt performance with the same models. Does not help if the limits make the entire tool utterly useless, does it?

title: Rubber Duck setting should force relaunch of application

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: agent-host
labels.description: Issues for the agent-host implementation (not the protocol)

body:
Testing https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/319092

In the current state of the rubber duck setting it only works after fully restarting the application. In that case we should prompt the user to restart the window when they change the setting. We do this for other settings too like `window.titleBarStyle`.
This can be implemented in `\vs\workbench\contrib\relauncher\browser\relauncher.contribution.ts`

title: Support SQL Keyword Formatting Inside C# String Literals
body:
When writing SQL queries inside C# string literals (especially verbatim strings and raw string literals), it would be helpful if Visual Studio Code and C# tooling could recognize SQL content and provide:

* SQL syntax highlighting
* SQL IntelliSense
* Automatic SQL formatting
* SQL keyword capitalization (SELECT, FROM, WHERE, JOIN, etc.)
* Validation of SQL syntax inside strings

Example:

var query = """
select *
from employee
where id = 1
""";

Expected behavior:

The editor recognizes the content as SQL and optionally formats it as:

SELECT *
FROM employee
WHERE id = 1;

This would significantly improve readability and productivity for developers who frequently embed SQL queries in C# applications.

title: Copilot Premium Request Usage Appears Excessive Despite All Models Showing 1x Multiplier
body: Hi Copilot Team, I would like to report a potential issue regarding Premium Request consumption. Issue Description All available models in Copilot currently display a 1x multiplier in the model selector. However, after using Copilot for only a single morning session (a few hours of normal coding and chat usage), my Premium Requests usage already reached 37.5% of the monthly allowance. This consumption rate seems disproportionately high compared to the displayed 1x cost multiplier. Environment Copilot Agent Mode Workspace Index: Basic Index Models tested include Claude, Gemini, and GPT series, all showing 1x consumption. Expected Behavior If a model is labeled as 1x, the premium request consumption should be consistent with that multiplier and usage should be reasonably predictable. Actual Behavior Premium Request allowance decreased to approximately 37.5% used after only a short period of usage. It is unclear whether: Agent Mode is consuming additional hidden requests, Background indexing/tool usage is counted separately, Or the displayed 1x multiplier does not accurately reflect actual consumption. Screenshot Attached The attached screenshot shows all available models marked as 1x while Premium Request usage has already reached 37.5%. Could you please clarify how Premium Requests are being calculated in Agent Mode and investigate whether the usage tracking is functioning correctly? Thank you.

title: [BYOK] VS Code Copilot Chat: "Sorry, your request failed" – Server error, Stream terminated – Client Request Id 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633
body:
## Summary

When using GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code with **Bring Your Own Key (BYOK)**, I consistently get a server error and the request fails, even after retries.

**Error message:**
```text
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Client Request Id: 3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633

Reason: Server error. Stream terminated: Error: Server error. Stream terminated
at dG._provideLanguageModelResponse
  (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:16552)
at process.processTicksAndRejections
  (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5)
at async dG.provideLanguageModelResponse
  (/Applications/Visual Studio Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/extensions/copilot/dist/extension.js:1710:17555)
```

This happens repeatedly and prevents me from using Copilot Chat with my BYOK model.

---

## Environment

- **OS:** macOS (version: [e.g. macOS 15.5])
- **VS Code version:** [e.g. 1.106.0] (check via `Code > About Visual Studio Code`)
- **GitHub Copilot extension version:** [e.g. 1.260.0] (check in Extensions panel)
- **Copilot Chat extension version:** [if separate, e.g. 0.26.0]
- **Copilot mode:** BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
- **Model / provider:** [e.g. GPT-4o via OpenAI, or your exact model name and provider]
- **VS Code installation:** [e.g. official .pkg from code.visualstudio.com, or from Homebrew]

---

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code on macOS.
2. Ensure I’m signed in with my GitHub account that has Copilot access.
3. Configure Copilot Chat to use **BYOK** with my model: [briefly describe, e.g. “OpenAI API key in settings” or “provider URL and key”].
4. Open the **Copilot Chat** panel.
5. Type a simple prompt, e.g. “Explain this code” or “Generate a function to …”.
6. Send the request.

**Expected behavior:**  
Copilot returns a response.

**Actual behavior:**  
Copilot returns the error above and the stream terminates.

---

## Frequency

- **How often:** [e.g. 100% of the time / every request / intermittently]
- **Since when:** [e.g. started on 2026-05-28, or “since I started using BYOK”]

---

## What I’ve Tried

- Retrying the request multiple times in Copilot Chat [🔄 retry button].
- Starting a **new chat** in Copilot Chat.
- Switching to a different model in the Copilot Chat model selector (if available).
- Reloading VS Code: `Developer: Reload Window`.
- Checking network connection (stable, no proxy / or describe if you use a proxy).
- Running: **GitHub Copilot: Collect Diagnostics** and saving the file.
- Setting log level to **Trace** for **GitHub Copilot** and **GitHub Copilot Chat**, then checking the Output panel.

Nothing has resolved the issue.

---

## Diagnostics & Logs

I have collected:

- **Diagnostics file:** `github-copilot-diagnostics-*.json` (attached)
- **Copilot Chat logs** from the Output panel (`GitHub Copilot Chat` channel), including the full stack trace with the Client Request Id.
- **Client Request Id:** `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633`

I can paste redacted logs here or attach them if GitHub allows file uploads.

---

## Additional Context

- I am using **BYOK** to connect to my own model provider.
- The same API key/model works fine when tested directly (e.g., via curl / Postscript / other tool): [yes/no – if yes, briefly describe].
- This appears to be a **server-side stream termination** error in the Copilot extension, not a local code issue.

---

## Request

Could you please:

1. Investigate whether this is a known issue with **Copilot Chat + BYOK** on macOS.
2. Check logs for Client Request Id `3afc9c34-3437-4d34-a824-84a018149633` on the server side.
3. Provide guidance on:
   - Any required configuration for BYOK in VS Code.
   - Whether this is due to a service outage or a bug in the Copilot extension.

title: Test: Copilot Chat offers Ubuntu 24.04 bubblewrap sandbox repair
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#319061

- [x] Linux @aeschli

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319256%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat detects when `bubblewrap` is installed but unusable on Ubuntu 24.04 because of AppArmor / user namespace restrictions, offers repair actions before running a sandboxed terminal command, re-checks sandbox prerequisites after repair, and does not silently fail or run the command before the sandbox is usable.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders on Ubuntu 24.04
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Use a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use a disposable VM or test machine because this test may install AppArmor packages and modify system sandbox policy
- Ensure Linux sandboxing is enabled:
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
- Ensure `bubblewrap` and `socat` are installed, but `bubblewrap` cannot create the sandbox namespace. For example, the following command should fail with a namespace / permission error such as `bwrap: setting up uid map: Permission denied`:
  ```sh
  bwrap --unshare-net --dev-bind / / echo ok
  ```

### Scenario 1: unusable bubblewrap offers repair and does not run the command first

1. Open a Copilot Chat agent mode session in the workspace.
2. Prompt:
   > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
3. When prompted, first choose **Cancel**.

**Expected:**
- A confirmation titled similar to **Repair Bubblewrap Sandbox** appears before the command is executed.
- The confirmation explains that bubblewrap is installed but cannot create the required sandbox namespace.
- After choosing **Cancel**, `echo sandbox-ok` is not run.
- Copilot Chat reports that the bubblewrap sandbox repair was cancelled, rather than showing a silent terminal failure.

### Scenario 2: AppArmor repair re-checks prerequisites and then runs sandboxed

1. Re-issue the same prompt:
   > Run `echo sandbox-ok` in the terminal.
2. Choose **Apply AppArmor Fix**.
3. If prompted for a sudo password, use the focused/revealed terminal to enter it.

**Expected:**
- VS Code runs the AppArmor remediation command in a terminal and waits for it to complete.
- After the remediation succeeds, VS Code re-checks sandbox prerequisites before running the original command.
- The original `echo sandbox-ok` command then runs and completes successfully inside the sandbox.
- Copilot Chat shows `sandbox-ok` in the result.

### Scenario 3: repaired sandbox works on the next command

1. In the same workspace, prompt Copilot Chat agent mode again:
   > Run `echo sandbox-still-ok` in the terminal.

**Expected:**
- No bubblewrap repair prompt is shown.
- The command runs in the sandbox and returns `sandbox-still-ok`.

title: Test: Copilot Chat retries network-blocked commands inside the sandbox
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#319181

- [x] macOS @jruales
- [x] Linux @rzhao271 

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319254%0A%0A&assignees=dileepyavan)

---

Verify that Copilot Chat terminal commands that need network access first retry inside the sandbox with unrestricted network access after user confirmation, instead of immediately falling back to unsandboxed execution.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- Open a trusted workspace folder
- Use Copilot Chat in agent mode
- Use macOS or Linux (Windows sandboxing currently enables sandboxing with network access, so this network-blocking scenario is not applicable there)
- Ensure the sandbox is enabled but network is **not** globally allowed:
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `true` (default)
  - `chat.agent.sandbox.allowUnsandboxedCommands` = `true` (default)
  - Do **not** add the test domain below to the sandbox network allowlist
- Open a **Copilot Chat** agent mode session in the workspace and keep it open for the whole test

### Scenario 1: blocked network command requests allow-network sandbox confirmation

1. In the Copilot Chat agent mode session, prompt:
   > Run `git fetch origin`.

**Expected:**
- VS Code either detects the blocked domain before running, or the first sandboxed attempt is blocked by network restrictions.
- Copilot Chat retries by requesting to run the command sandboxed with unrestricted network access (`requestAllowNetwork`) and provides a reason.
- The confirmation/details mention unrestricted network access in the sandbox.
- The command remains sandboxed; the confirmation is **not** for unsandboxed execution.
- After approving, the command succeeds and shows an HTTP status line (for example `HTTP/2 200` or `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`).

### Scenario 2: disabling allow-network retry uses the old unsandboxed fallback

1. Set `chat.agent.sandbox.retryWithAllowNetworkRequests` = `false`.
2. Start a new Copilot Chat agent mode session in the same workspace.
3. Re-issue the same prompt as in Scenario 1.

**Expected:**
- Copilot Chat does **not** request `requestAllowNetwork` / unrestricted network access inside the sandbox.
- If the command cannot run because of sandbox network restrictions, the fallback confirmation is for unsandboxed execution.
- The command does **not** silently run outside the sandbox without confirmation.

title: [sandbox]Retry using allowNetwork option before retrying outside the sandbox

labels.name: unreleased
labels.description: Patch has not yet been released in VS Code Insiders

type.name: Feature
type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality

body: Currently when a command fails in sandbox, it gets retried outside the sandbox. If  a command fails due to network issues, agent should be able to retry inside the sandbox with allowNetwork option.

title: Test: Copilot CLI sandbox blocks reads outside the workspace
body:
Refs: microsoft/vscode#317981

- [x] Windows @kycutler
- [ ] macOS @alexdima
- [x] Linux @rzhao271

Complexity: 3


[Create Issue](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/new?template=blank&body=Testing+%23319218%0A%0A&assignees=chrmarti)

---

Verify that the Copilot CLI session's sandbox prevents the agent from reading files outside the workspace on all three platforms, and that toggling the sandbox setting takes effect within the same chat session.

### Prerequisites

- Latest VS Code Insiders
- Sign in to GitHub Copilot
- A workspace folder that does **not** contain `~/something.txt` (i.e. your home directory is outside the open folder)
- Create a small file with recognizable content at `~/something.txt` (use `%USERPROFILE%\something.txt` on Windows), e.g.:
  - macOS / Linux: `echo "SECRET-CONTENT-12345" > ~/something.txt`
  - Windows (PowerShell): `Set-Content -Path $HOME\something.txt -Value "SECRET-CONTENT-12345"`
- Start with the sandbox **off**:
  - macOS / Linux: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `off`
  - Windows: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabledWindows` = `off`
- Leave `chat.agent.sandbox.fileSystem` at defaults (do not allow-list the home directory)
- Open a **Copilot CLI** chat session in the workspace and keep it open for the whole test

### Scenario 1: sandbox off — read succeeds

1. In the Copilot CLI chat, prompt:
   > Run `cat ~/something.txt` in the shell and show me its output. On Windows use `Get-Content $HOME\something.txt`.

**Expected:** the command succeeds and `SECRET-CONTENT-12345` appears in the response.

### Scenario 2: turn sandbox on in the same session — read is blocked

1. Without closing the chat, switch the sandbox setting on for the current platform:
   - macOS / Linux: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabled` = `on`
   - Windows: `chat.agent.sandbox.enabledWindows` = `allowNetwork` (sandbox on Windows currently requires `allowNetwork`)
2. In the same chat session, re-issue the same prompt as in Scenario 1.

**Expected:**
- The command runs inside the sandbox.
- The read fails with a sandbox / permission-denied style error (wording varies by backend: macOS Seatbelt `Operation not permitted`, Linux LXC `Permission denied`, Windows MXC sandbox `Access is denied`).
- `SECRET-CONTENT-12345` does **not** appear in the response.

title: Test: Extensions delayed auto-update
body:
Refs: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/7834, https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-internalbacklog/issues/7769

- [ ] anyOS @isidorn
- [ ] anyOS

Complexity: 4

Author: @sandy081

---

## Context

This TPI covers the new `delayed` value for the `extensions.autoUpdate` setting. The setting is now a string enum (`on` | `delayed` | `off`).

When set to `delayed`, an extension whose newer version was published less than 6 hours ago is treated as outdated-but-delayed: it is excluded from auto-update and the activity badge, and surfaces an info status explaining when it will be auto updated. Manual updates still work.

## Setup

1. Latest VS Code Insiders.
2. A few installed extensions, some with recent updates available.

> **Tip — testing the 6-hour delay:** The easiest way is to **change the system clock forward by 6+ hours and reload the window** — the next auto-update check will then see the extension as past its delay. Alternatively, find two real extensions in the Marketplace: one with a version published in the last few hours (delayed path), and one with an update older than 6 hours (normal path).

## What to test

### 1. Migration of legacy values

`extensions.autoUpdate` is an **application-scope** setting, so migration runs once per installation regardless of profile/workspace. Start with any of the **old** values set, then launch the new Insiders and verify the value is migrated/normalized on startup.

| Old value in `settings.json` | Expected migrated value |
| --- | --- |
| `true` | `"on"` |
| `"all"` | `"on"` |
| `"onlyEnabledExtensions"` | `"on"` |
| `false` | `"off"` |
| `"none"` | `"off"` |
| `"onlySelectedExtensions"` | `"off"` |

For each:
1. Set the old value in `settings.json` and reload.
2. Verify the value is rewritten to the new enum.
3. Verify the auto-update behavior matches the new value (on / off).

### 2. Setting UI

1. Open Settings UI and search for `extensions.autoUpdate`.
2. Verify it is rendered as a dropdown with three options: **On**, **Delayed**, **Off**. Read the description of each option and validate that it clearly and accurately explains the behavior.

### 3. `on` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `on`.
2. Verify outdated extensions are auto-updated.
3. Disable an outdated extension and verify it is **not** auto-updated — only enabled extensions should be auto-updated.

### 4. `delayed` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `delayed`.
2. For an extension whose newer version was published **< 6 hours ago**:
   - It is **not** auto-updated.
   - It is **not** counted in the activity bar badge.
   - It shows an info status in the **Extensions view** explaining **when** it will be auto updated.
   - Open the extension in the **extension editor** and verify the same info status is shown.
   - Hover the extension and verify the same info is rendered correctly in the hover (no stale info, no flicker).
   - Clicking the "Update" button still updates it manually.
3. For an extension whose newer version was published **> 6 hours ago**:
   - It is auto-updated normally and counted in the badge.
4. Disable an outdated extension and verify it is **not** auto-updated — only enabled extensions should be auto-updated.

### 5. `off` behavior

1. Set `extensions.autoUpdate` to `off`.
2. Verify extensions are not auto-updated.
3. Manual update still works.

title: Terminal Shell Integration requires update for newer PowerShell 7.4+ ConstrainedLanguage AUDIT mode

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Issues identified as good community contribution opportunities

labels.name: terminal-shell-integration
labels.description: Shell integration infrastructure, command decorations, etc.

labels.name: terminal-shell-pwsh
labels.description: An issue in the terminal specific to PowerShell, including shell integration

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Work not yet planned for a specific release.

body:
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version:  1.107.0
- OS Version:  Windows 11 25H2

## Steps to Reproduce:

1. On Windows 11, enable a WDAC / App Control **audit-only** policy (On newer systems this is slowly being automatically enabled)
2. Install PowerShell **7.4 or later**. It will run in `ConstrainedLanguage AUDIT` Mode
3. Launch VS Code
4. Open the integrated terminal using `pwsh` profile
5. Observe that PowerShell terminal shell integration does not initialize (prompt tracking / command boundaries unavailable)

## Expected Result:

Shell integration should initialize when:
- `$ExecutionContext.SessionState.LanguageMode` is `FullLanguage`, or
- `$ExecutionContext.SessionState.LanguageMode` is `ConstrainedLanguage` **and** the system lockdown policy is `Audit`
(Refer to Additional Information below for more details as to why)

Shell integration should continue to be blocked when constrained language mode is **enforced**. (However it would be nice if the integration explicitly states that it is being blocked due to a restricted language mode - something that is not super evident to unfamiliar devs).

## Actual Result:

- PowerShell runs normally, in unrestricted fashion
- Scripts execute without restriction. For example, dynamic code (e.g. `Add-Type`) works fine
- However, Terminal shell integration does not initialize because it **assumes** restricted shell
- VS Code reports terminal integration failure, despite a fully functional shell
- This issue also affects downstream IDEs that reuse VS Code’s terminal integration logic (e.g. Windsurf, Cursor).

A minimal fix that allows shell integration in `ConstrainedLanguage + Audit mode`, while continuing to block other enforced restricted language modes, is being prepared as a follow-up PR and should be up soon.

## Additional Information:

PowerShell **7.4** (released Nov 2023) introduced **Constrained Language Audit Mode**.

In this mode:
- `LanguageMode` reports `ConstrainedLanguage`
- But runtime semantics are equivalent to `FullLanguage`
- No restrictions are actually enforced
- The mode exists solely to log what *would* be blocked under enforcement

Audit vs enforced CLM can be detected via:

```powershell
[System.Management.Automation.Security.SystemPolicy]::GetSystemLockdownPolicy()
```

The current language mode guard appears to originate from: #158548

That change predates the introduction of audit-only constrained language mode and was totally correct at the time. Since PowerShell semantics have evolved, the same guard now produces false negatives in audit-only scenarios by treating all `ConstrainedLanguage` sessions as enforced.

It is also worth noting that WDAC / App Control audit policies can become active implicitly on Windows 11 without explicit user configuration. Common cases include:
1) Activation of the `Verified and Reputable Desktop` baseline policy on consumer, developer-mode enabled machines not enrolled in any kind of MDM (think freelance/solo devs or small teams).
2) PowerShell Developer-oriented configurations where Windows prefers audit-first behavior instead of enforcement of PS1 script-breaking policies
3) OEM or security tooling that deploys audit policies as part of a hardening baseline, to meet Copilot+ PC certifications for example
4) User Toggling of advanced platform features (e.g. Copilot+ / Recall–class features), and connected VBS and related security protections

As a result, audit-only `ConstrainedLanguage` mode is becoming increasingly common on modern Windows 11 systems. In its defense, this is just `FullLanguage` mode with extra logs and accountability and should be treated as such.

title: Prompt-cache dip: system prompt mutates mid-conversation when async-gated skills (e.g. chronicle) become visible

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
The system prompt is rebuilt from scratch on every turn and includes extension-contributed skills filtered by their `when` clause against the live context-key state. When a context key flips mid-conversation (e.g. due to async experimentation/treatment fetches or a delayed `setContext`), a skill can be inserted into the `<skills>` block on a later turn, invalidating the system-level prompt-cache breakpoint.
## Root cause

The chronicle skill is gated on a context key that is fed from an experiment-based config:

- `extensions/copilot/package.json` — `{ "path": "./assets/prompts/skills/chronicle/SKILL.md", "when": "github.copilot.sessionSearch.enabled" }`
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/contextKeys/vscode-node/contextKeys.contribution.ts:88-90` — wires the context key from `ConfigKey.LocalIndexEnabled` (an `ExperimentBased` setting that defaults to `false`)
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/service/promptsServiceImpl.ts:516-530` — filters extension-contributed skills against `contextKeyService.contextMatchesRules(when)` on every list call, and invalidates its cache when any tracked `when`-key changes (line 173-174).

title: Feature Request: Context menu for priority management in GitHub Copilot queued items
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

*Currently, managing the execution order within the GitHub Copilot queue lacks an agile mechanism. When a developer needs to reorganize the priority of queued Copilot tasks or elements, the UI does not provide a frictionless way to reorder them, which slows down the workflow and makes dynamic prioritization cumbersome.*

I request the implementation of a context menu (accessible via right-click) specifically on the "Queued" items within the GitHub Copilot interface. This menu should expose the following sorting directives:

* **Move Up (Higher priority):** Shifts the item to the position immediately above it in the queue.
* **Move Down (Lower priority):** Shifts the item to the position immediately below it.
* **Move to Top (Highest priority):** Assigns critical priority, elevating the item to the very beginning of the queue (index 0).
* **Move to Bottom (Lowest priority):** Minimizes priority, sending the item to the end of the current backlog.

**Alternatives considered:**

* **On-hover directional arrows:** I evaluated using up/down arrows visible on `:hover`. However, this approach introduces a UI/UX conflict, as an arrow icon is already currently utilized to promote a "queued" item directly into the active state. A context menu avoids interface clutter and prevents accidental misclicks.
* **Drag and Drop:** Adding Drag and Drop support is a viable alternative. However, a context menu provides faster and more deterministic interaction, which is especially critical for direct jump actions (Move to Top / Move to Bottom), eliminating the need for tedious scrolling through extensive lists.

Type: Bug When you have a Python function annotated with an decorator (e.g. `@app` or `@pytest.fixture`), /doc is adding the docstrings before the function definition rather than after. title: Re-enable multifile-edit-claude simulation tests with a supported Claude model

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
The `-claude` variant in `extensions/copilot/test/inline/multiFileEdit.stest.ts` was temporarily disabled in #311003 because `CHAT_MODEL.CLAUDE_SONNET` (`claude-3.5-sonnet`) now returns `model_not_supported` from the endpoint, breaking simulation cache regen.

### To do
- Update `CHAT_MODEL.CLAUDE_SONNET` (or pick a different constant) to point at a currently-supported Claude model, e.g. `claude-sonnet-4`.
- Re-enable the call in `multiFileEdit.stest.ts` (search for the `// Temporarily disabled` comment).
- Regenerate the simulation cache for the `multifile-edit-claude` suite.
- Update the test family name in baseline.json if the model id changes.

### Context
- Main currently passes this test only because the salt is old and stale cache entries (from when the model still worked) satisfy `simulate-ci --require-cache`.
- #311003 bumped the cache salt and changed request shape (key ordering), invalidating those entries, exposing the underlying model_not_supported error.

title: Feature request: add setting to disable sensitive input detection in terminal agent tools

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: author-verification-requested
labels.description: Issues potentially verifiable by issue author

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

## feature request: add a setting to disable sensitive input detection in terminal agent tools

### problem

when using copilot agent mode to run automated deploy scripts that execute commands over ssh on remote devices (e.g. raspberry pi), the `OutputMonitor` detects `[sudo] password for ...` in the terminal output and triggers `onDidDetectSensitiveInputNeeded`, which auto-cancels the command in autopilot mode.

this happens even when the password is piped non-interactively via `sudo -S` (e.g. `echo mypassword | sudo -S systemctl restart myservice`). there is no actual interactive prompt, but sudo still prints `[sudo] password for user:` to stderr before reading from stdin, and the output monitor catches that line.

the behavior is also inconsistent: the same script sometimes passes and sometimes gets blocked, depending on timing (whether the stderr line arrives before or after sudo reads from stdin).

the suggested workarounds (nopasswd sudo, ssh keys, running manually) don't always apply:
- nopasswd sudo may not be allowed by the device security policy
- ssh keys are already in place for the ssh connection. the issue is `sudo` inside the remote session
- running manually defeats the purpose of agent-driven automation

### proposed solution

add a boolean setting, for example `chat.tools.terminal.blockSensitiveInput` (default: `true`), that controls whether the sensitive input elicitation is registered. when set to `false`, the `_registerSensitiveInputElicitation` method in `runInTerminalTool.ts` would return an empty disposable without registering the `onDidDetectSensitiveInputNeeded` listener.

this is a minimal change: just one early-return check at the top of the method, plus the setting registration in the configuration schema.

i have already tested this patch on vscode 1.120.0 (commit `0958016b2a`, 2026-05-12) on windows 11, and it works as expected. the default behavior is fully preserved, and only users who explicitly opt out are affected.

### related discussion

https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/196006#discussioncomment-16987051

### additional context

the relevant source code is in:
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/terminalContrib/chatAgentTools/browser/tools/runInTerminalTool.ts` (the `_registerSensitiveInputElicitation` method)

title: Integrated terminal: VS Code injects installation root into process PATH, code CLI resolves to Code.exe instead of code.cmd

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: install-update
labels.description: VS Code installation and upgrade system issues

labels.name: windows
labels.description: VS Code on Windows issues

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
﻿### Problem

VS Code injects `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at an early position in the integrated terminal's process PATH. This causes `code` to resolve to `Code.exe` (the GUI binary) instead of `code.cmd` in the `bin\` subfolder.

Every `code` CLI invocation (`code --version`, `code --list-extensions`, etc.) opens a **new VS Code window** instead of executing in the terminal.

### Version

- VS Code: 1.115.0 (system setup)
- OS: Windows 11 (10.0.26100)
- Not running as Administrator
- Compatibility Run as admin is NOT set on Code.exe

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Install VS Code system-wide (default: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code`)
2. System PATH correctly contains only `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin`
3. Open VS Code integrated terminal (PowerShell)
4. Run: `env:PATH -split ';' | Where-Object { _ -match 'VS Code' }`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code` at position 2
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin` at position 33
5. Run: `Get-Command code | Select-Object Source`
   - Shows `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` (wrong!)
6. Run: `code --version` - opens a new VS Code window instead of printing the version

### Expected Behavior

`code` should resolve to `code.cmd` in `bin\`, which sets ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 and runs cli.js correctly.

### Root Cause

VS Code prepends its installation directory (containing Code.exe) to the terminal's process PATH, shadowing the correct `bin\code.cmd`.

### Impact on Copilot Agent Mode

GitHub Copilot agent mode uses run_in_terminal to execute `code --list-extensions` and similar CLI commands. Each invocation spawns a new VS Code window without the user's consent. Especially disruptive during automated multi-step tasks.

### Workaround

In settings.json:
`terminal.integrated.env.windows`: { `PATH`: `C:\Program Files\Microsoft VS Code\bin;env:PATH` }

This prepends bin\ so code.cmd takes priority over the injected Code.exe.

### Note

This is NOT the admin/elevated terminal issue (#91613, fixed in 1.110). This occurs in a normal non-elevated terminal session.

body:
As soon as the user clicks the 🔍 icon in the session list, the order of sessions changes — causing a disruptive visual jump before any text has been typed.

The session order should only change once the first character is typed (as we do in the quick pick). This would provide a smoother UX.

title: MCP Server `gallery` Field Not Saved During Protocol Installation

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-mcp-management
labels.description: Issues related to browsing and managing MCP Servers in VS Code

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->


## Description

When installing an MCP server via the `vscode:mcp/install` protocol URL, the `gallery` field is incorrectly removed from the server configuration and not saved to `mcp.json`. This prevents VS Code from:
- Fetching server metadata from the registry
- Displaying proper server information
- Checking for updates
- Managing server enablement based on gallery status

## Root Cause

In `src/vs/workbench/contrib/mcp/browser/mcpWorkbenchService.ts` at line 675, the `handleMcpInstallUri` method destructures the `gallery` field out of the configuration object but never uses it:

```typescript
const { name, inputs, gallery, ...config } = parsed;
//                      ^^^^^^^ extracted but never used
```

This removes `gallery` from the `config` object that gets passed to the installation process, so it's never written to `mcp.json`.

## Expected Behavior

The `gallery` field should be preserved in the MCP server configuration since it's a valid field defined in `IMcpServerConfiguration` interface (`src/vs/platform/mcp/common/mcpPlatformTypes.ts` line 32):

```typescript
export interface ICommonMcpServerConfiguration {
    readonly type: McpServerType;
    readonly version?: string;
    readonly gallery?: boolean | string;  // Valid configuration field
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open VS Code
2. Create a protocol URL with a `gallery` field:
   ```
   vscode:mcp/install?%7B%22name%22%3A%22test-server%22%2C%22url%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%2Fmcp%22%2C%22type%22%3A%22http%22%2C%22gallery%22%3A%22https%3A%2F%2Fexample.com%2Fregistry%2Fservers%2Ftest%2Flatest%22%7D
   ```
   
   Decoded JSON:
   ```json
   {
     "name": "test-server",
     "url": "https://example.com/mcp",
     "type": "http",
     "gallery": "https://example.com/registry/servers/test/latest"
   }
   ```

3. Click the protocol URL or navigate to it in a browser
4. VS Code opens and processes the installation
5. Open `mcp.json` file (location varies by OS):
   - **macOS**: `~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/mcp.json`
   - **Windows**: `%APPDATA%\Code\User\mcp.json`
   - **Linux**: `~/.config/Code/User/mcp.json`

## Actual Result

The `gallery` field is missing from the saved configuration:

```json
{
  "servers": {
    "test-server": {
      "url": "https://example.com/mcp",
      "type": "http"
    }
  }
}
```

## Expected Result

The `gallery` field should be preserved:

```json
{
  "servers": {
    "test-server": {
      "url": "https://example.com/mcp",
      "type": "http",
      "gallery": "https://example.com/registry/servers/test/latest"
    }
  }
}
```

## Proposed Fix

Remove `gallery` from the destructuring assignment so it remains in the `config` object:

**Current (buggy) code:**
```typescript
const { name, inputs, gallery, ...config } = parsed;
```

**Fixed code:**
```typescript
const { name, inputs, ...config } = parsed;
```

This allows the `gallery` field to remain in the `config` object and be properly saved to `mcp.json`.

## Impact

This bug affects any MCP server installation that uses the protocol handler with a `gallery` URL, preventing VS Code from properly managing gallery-registered servers and accessing their metadata.

## Version Information

- VS Code Version: [Your version]
- OS: [Your OS]
- MCP Feature: Enabled

## Additional Context

The `gallery` field is documented as a valid configuration option and is used throughout the codebase to determine server enablement state (see `getEnablementStatus` method in the same file) and sync with gallery metadata.

title: run_in_terminal opens new empty VS Code window instead of running in current window

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: install-update
labels.description: VS Code installation and upgrade system issues

labels.name: windows
labels.description: VS Code on Windows issues

body:
Summary

When the Copilot Chat agent invokes the run_in_terminal tool, it opens a new empty VS Code window (as if "New Window" was triggered) instead of creating a terminal session in the current window. The terminal itself does appear in the original window's Terminal panel, but as a hidden terminal requiring manual focus.

Environment

VS Code: 1.116.0 (Windows 11)
GitHub Copilot Chat: 0.45.1
OS: Windows 11, terminal profile: Git Bash
Workspace: single-root
Reproduction Steps

Open any workspace in VS Code
Switch Copilot Chat to Agent mode
Ask the agent to run any terminal command (e.g., git status)
Observe a new empty VS Code window opening
In the original window, observe "1 Hidden Terminal" in the Terminal panel instead of an auto-focused terminal
Expected Behavior

The terminal command runs in the current VS Code window, with the new terminal visible and focused in the Terminal panel — no new window opens.

Actual Behavior

A new empty VS Code window is spawned on every run_in_terminal invocation. The terminal is created in the original window but is hidden (requires clicking "1 Hidden Terminal" to show).

Workaround

Use send_to_terminal with terminalId to reuse an existing terminal. If all terminals are busy, ask the user to open a new one manually.

Additional Context

Setting "terminal.integrated.focusAfterLaunch": "always" in workspace settings.json resolves the hidden terminal symptom but does not prevent the new window from opening.

title: Feature Request: Local AI in Offline Networks

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->
I would like to request a feature that allows the use of AI functionalities in an air-gapped or restricted network environment. Currently, our corporate intranet does not have access to the internet and cannot connect to github.com for authentication or external API calls.

We would like the ability to use AI features by connecting to a local Ollama instance hosted within our internal network. Even when the IDE or application is unable to log in to GitHub, it should provide an option to:

- Skip the external login/internet check.
- Manually configure a local API endpoint.
- Select and utilize models pulled via the local Ollama service.

Thank you for considering this request.

title: Allow Pin or Favorite Branches in Git Checkout Panel

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: git
labels.description: GIT issues

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Work not yet planned for a specific release.

body: ### Feature Request In the Git: Checkout to... QuickPick, it would be very useful to be able to pin or visually distinguish important branches such as `main`, `develop`, or long-living release branches. ### Problem In repositories with many branches, important branches get lost in the list. Users frequently need to scroll or search for `main` / `develop`, even though these branches are used constantly. ### Proposed Solution - Allow pinning/favoriting branches so they always appear at the top - Allow simple visual distinction (icon, color, or group header) ### Why this matters This would significantly improve daily workflow for users working with many branches and aligns with features available in other popular IDEs.

title: Deferred custom tools can be uncallable by agents (GPT 5.4)

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified

labels.name: insiders-released

body:
Type: Bug Since a couple of days ago, my custom extension's Agent Helper Kit's shell tools cannot be called by the chat agent anymore. The tools are correctly defined in their package.json, and have been working extremely well so far. Since friday, the chat agent has become unable to call them. These tools are no longer listed in the Metadata object (before the system prompt). Instead they are in availableDeferredTools: ``` < availableDeferredTools> Available deferred tools (must be loaded with tool_search before use): await_shell create_directory get_changed_files get_last_shell_command get_shell_command get_shell_output github_repo github_text_search kill_shell run_in_shell run_vscode_command send_to_shell < /availableDeferredTools> ``` When the agent tries to load them, their schemas are not available. This is the tool_search call response: ``` ["run_in_shell","run_vscode_command","await_shell","mcp_pylance_mcp_s_pylanceRunCodeSnippet","get_last_shell_command"] ``` This does nothing for the agent. It does not know how to use them. For example, it tries to invoke like this: ``` id : call_y0qNQxSs8mHzJZBHyIZVP5Lj__vscode-1778401017699 tool : run_in_shell args : { "command": "yarn --version", "cwd": "~/Sites/agent-helper-kit", "explanation": "Check the installed Yarn version through the shell tool.", "goal": "Verify shell tool invocation works and report Yarn's version.", "riskAssessment": "Low risk: read-only version check with no file modifications, no secret handling, no network access expected, and no destructive side effects.", "riskAssessmentContext": [ "Command: yarn --version" ], "shell": "", "columns": 240, "full_output": true, "last_lines": 0, "regex": "", "regex_flags": "", "timeout": 0 } ``` But this fails because some of those fields are mutually exclusive, which it would know if it had the schema like it used to. The agent even correctly recognizes the issues (invalid and too many fields), and "thinks" it's attempting a call with a minimal fieldset, but ends up calling it the same way. It does this repeatedly.
Here's a chat debug log of the above happening. 🚨 Note: This log may contain personal information such as the contents of your files or terminal output. Please review the contents carefully before sharing. panel/editAgent - c125bc29 Request Messages System User Response Metadata requestType : ChatResponses model : gpt-5.4 maxPromptTokens : 271997 maxResponseTokens: 128000 location : 7 otherOptions : {"stream":true,"store":false} reasoning : {"effort":"high","summary":"detailed"} intent : undefined startTime : 2026-05-10T08:21:56.728Z endTime : 2026-05-10T08:21:57.817Z duration : 1089ms ourRequestId : 2cc7e6b3-e49d-40e3-8780-b5fbabda2611 lastResponseId :  using gpt-5.4 tools (17) : tool_search, apply_patch, create_file, fetch_webpage, file_search, grep_search, get_errors, list_dir, memory, read_file, semantic_search, view_image, vscode_askQuestions, vscode_listCodeUsages, vscode_renameSymbol, manage_todo_list, runSubagent Request Messages System [TRUNCATED, IRRELEVANT] < instructions> < attachment filePath="/Users/luismiguelsousa/Sites/agent-helper-kit/.github/instructions/development.instructions.md"> [TRUNCATED, IRRELEVANT] < /attachment> < attachment filePath="/Users/luismiguelsousa/.claude/CLAUDE.md"> [TRUNCATED, IRRELEVANT] < /attachment> < attachment filePath="/Users/luismiguelsousa/Sites/agent-helper-kit/AGENTS.md"> [TRUNCATED, IRRELEVANT] < /attachment> < instructions> Here is a list of instruction files that contain rules for working with this codebase. title: Auto-completion disabled for languages with ID >= 256 due to LanguageID bits overlapping with TokenType bits
body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes/No - VS Code Version: 1.92.2 - OS Version: I have discovered an issue in vscode's LineTokens metadata generation, specifically related to the 8-bit limit for LanguageID. According to the official blog ([Syntax Highlighting Optimizations](https://code.visualstudio.com/blogs/2017/02/08/syntax-highlighting-optimizations#_tokens-theme-matching)), vscode utilizes the first 8 bits of the metadata integer for LanguageID and bits 9-11 for TokenType. In getDefaultMetadata, the value is constructed as follows: ```TypeScript // vscode/src/vs/editor/common/tokens/contiguousTokensStore.ts public getTokens(topLevelLanguageId: string, lineIndex: number, lineText: string): LineTokens { let rawLineTokens: Uint32Array | ArrayBuffer | null = null; if (lineIndex < this._len) { rawLineTokens = this._lineTokens[lineIndex]; } if (rawLineTokens !== null && rawLineTokens !== EMPTY_LINE_TOKENS) { return new LineTokens(toUint32Array(rawLineTokens), lineText, this._languageIdCodec); } const lineTokens = new Uint32Array(2); lineTokens[0] = lineText.length; lineTokens[1] = getDefaultMetadata(this._languageIdCodec.encodeLanguageId(topLevelLanguageId)); return new LineTokens(lineTokens, lineText, this._languageIdCodec); } ... function getDefaultMetadata(topLevelLanguageId) { return ((topLevelLanguageId << MetadataConsts.LANGUAGEID_OFFSET) // <- here | (StandardTokenType.Other << MetadataConsts.TOKEN_TYPE_OFFSET) | ... ) >>> 0; } ``` ### Reproduction When the number of registered languages exceeds 255 (the 8-bit limit), topLevelLanguageId values for subsequent languages will overflow. Because topLevelLanguageId is shifted and bitwise OR-ed with StandardTokenType, this overflow causes a carry-over into the bits reserved for TokenType. For example, a LanguageID of 325 (binary 1 0100 0101) effectively truncates/overflows into the TokenType bit field, make the metadata becomes to `xxxx 001 0100 0101`, which should be `xxxx 000 (1)0100 0101`. A StandardTokenType.Others (0) bit field is erroneously modified to StandardTokenType.Comments (1) due to this bit carry-over. ### Actual Since quickSuggestion logic relies on TokenType being Other to trigger by default, this metadata corruption causes auto-completion to be silently disabled for all languages with an ID >= 256. ### Expected Even though it might not be a long-term goal to support more than 255 languages in a single instance, this behavior is a silent failure that leads to difficult-to-debug issues. I propose implementing a bitwise mask or modulo operation on topLevelLanguageId (e.g., topLevelLanguageId & 0xFF) before shifting to ensure that overflow does not contaminate the TokenType field.

title: Custom Endpoint (BYOK) models disappear from model picker on VS Code restart - hasByokModels.lastKnown race condition

labels.name: unreleased
labels.description: Patch has not yet been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: new release
labels.description: Issues found in a recent release of VS Code

body:
## Describe the bug

After restarting VS Code, Custom Endpoint (BYOK) models that were previously added via Chat: Manage Language Models → Add Models → Custom Endpoint disappear from the model picker for several seconds, and sometimes never appear unless the user manually runs Developer: Reload Window.

This is caused by a startup timing race condition in the HasByokModelsContribution class.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Add a Custom Endpoint model (e.g., DeepSeek, or any OpenAI-compatible endpoint) via the Language Models editor
2. Verify the model works and appears in the model picker as expected
3. Close all VS Code windows and reopen VS Code
4. Observe: The model picker initially shows only Copilot models - the Custom Endpoint (local) option is missing
5. Wait 5-15 seconds - the Custom Endpoint model eventually appears (but sometimes doesn't at all)

## Expected Behavior

Custom Endpoint models that have been successfully configured should appear in the model picker immediately upon VS Code startup, without any delay.

## Actual Behavior

The `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` storage key is restored as `false` on every cold startup, causing the `github.copilot.hasByokModels` context key to be `false`. The model picker hides all non-Copilot vendor models until the extension registration completes and triggers `_update()`.

## Technical Analysis

After inspecting the source code at `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/hasByokModelsContribution.ts`, the issue is:

1. `_restore()` reads `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` from storage - this is `false` on first startup
2. Even though `chatLanguageModels.json` is already configured with valid Custom Endpoint models, the `hasByokModels` context key remains `false`
3. The `_update()` method only corrects this **after** `whenInstalledExtensionsRegistered()` completes
4. During this window, the model picker hides all BYOK models

The key logic in `_update()`:
```typescript
if (!this._extensionsRegistered) {
    if (this._contextKeyService.getContextKeyValue<boolean>(
        ChatContextKeys.nonCopilotLanguageModelsAreUserSelectable.key)) {
        this._setResult(true);
    }
    return;
}
```

The optimistic flip only triggers if `nonCopilotLanguageModelsAreUserSelectable` is set, which isnt always the case on cold start.

Additionally, for users behind an HTTP proxy (e.g. `http.proxy: http://localhost:65532`), the Custom Endpoint providers model validation may fail during startup if the proxy is not ready yet, causing the entire provider group to be silently dropped from the model cache.

## Environment

- VS Code Version: 1.122.1
- OS Version: Windows 11
- Extension: GitHub Copilot Chat 0.50.1
- Proxy configured: `http.proxy: http://localhost:65532`

## Additional Context

The `chat.hasByokModels.lastKnown` key is stored in `state.vscdb` (SQLite) at `%APPDATA%\Code\User\globalStorage\state.vscdb`. Manually setting this to `true` before restart forces the models to appear immediately, confirming the root cause is the stale `false` value.

Related code files:
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/hasByokModelsContribution.ts`
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/customEndpointProvider.ts`
- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/languageModels.ts`

body: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes Starting from 1.110.0 VS Code prepend PATH environment variable with its install directory, when started from start menu icon or from `Code.exe`. There are no issue with PATH environment variable, when VS Code started from `bin\code.cmd`. This is problematic as plain `code` will now resolve into `Code.exe` instead of `code.cmd`, when used in internal terminal, so things like `code --install-extension`, `code --diff` or `code --merge` will no longer work, as `Code.exe` did not process this command line parameters. I tested it on clean Windows install (VM with snapshot just after install) with restore to clean state before each version tested. I tested it on 1.113.0-insider it was not affected, but now 1.113.0 released and bug still here, so I presume it just does not affect Insider builds. - VS Code Version: 1.110.0, 1.111.0, 1.112.0, 1.113.0 - OS Version: 10.0.26200.8037 Steps to Reproduce: 1. Clean install of VS Code. 2. Start VS Code from start menu icon. Screenshot from 1.109.0: Screenshot from 1.110.0

title: Built-in Mermaid.js support for Markdown previews

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: markdown-ext
labels.description: The Markdown extension: language features and preview

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

The recently released [VS Code 1.109.0 supports Mermaid.js diagrams in chat responses](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_109#_mermaid-diagrams-in-chat-responses).

This is done via a tool `renderMermaidDiagram` that comes with the bundled extension [`mermaid-chat-features`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/tree/main/extensions/mermaid-chat-features).

Regular Markdown previews still don't support Mermaid.js diagrams. There were already numerous feature requests (e.g., #151262, #168123, #185819, #251616, #261759) to add Mermaid.js support to Markdown previews. However, they all have been declined with the reason that this should be done in an external extension instead, e.g., [Markdown Preview Mermaid Support](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=bierner.markdown-mermaid), even by the person who authored said extension, `mermaid-chat-features`, and the Markdown preview itself, [primarily citing the decoupling of new Mermaid features from VS Code releases](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/251616#issuecomment-3138183374).

With the recent (or not so recent, since `mermaid-chat-features` has been included in VS Code since 1.105.0 in an experimental state, see #266121) addition of built-in Mermaid.js support in chat responses, I don't understand why this argument holds anymore since `mermaid-chat-features` is also bundled with VS Code. The Markdown preview could reuse some of the code of `mermaid-chat-features` (probably after refactoring) to support Mermaid.js diagrams as well.

The feature request is to please consider bundling Mermaid.js support for Markdown previews as a built-in extension, just like `mermaid-chat-features`.

Mermaid.js is widely used (as seen by the addition `mermaid-chat-features` to VS Code, the large number of previous feature requests to add Markdown preview support (see above), support by [GitHub](https://docs.github.com/en/get-started/writing-on-github/working-with-advanced-formatting/creating-diagrams#creating-mermaid-diagrams) and [GitLab](https://docs.gitlab.com/user/markdown/#mermaid), 86k GitHub stars for the [Mermaid.js repository](https://github.com/mermaid-js/mermaid), etc.) and therefore, bundling Markdown previews in VS Code would make the usage of Mermaid.js easier and more accessible. Also, this might reduce code duplication between `mermaid-chat-features` and Markdown preview extensions like Markdown Preview Mermaid Support. Finally, keep in mind that [third-party extensions may be blocked by enterprise policies when working on company laptops](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/enterprise/extensions).

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Open a Markdown file with a Mermaid.js block, e.g.

   ````markdown
   ```mermaid
   graph TD;
       A-->B;
       A-->C;
       B-->D;
       C-->D;
   ```
   ````

2. Run the command _Markdown: Open Preview_.

**Actual behavior:** A code block renders with the literal Mermaid.js code.

**Expected behavior:** A diagram renders.

title: chat plugins: Claude-format plugin frontmatter (rules `paths`, agents `tools`/`model`) is not transformed when installed via marketplace or pluginLocations
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.118.0 (also reproduces on current `main`, commit 70d194f98bc)
- OS Version: macOS 15 / Darwin 25.4.0 arm64 (cross-platform; not OS-specific)

## Summary

VS Code already detects whether an installed agent plugin is Copilot-format or Claude-format via `detectPluginFormat()` in `src/vs/platform/agentPlugins/common/pluginParsers.ts`. However, that detection result is only used to locate the manifest (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json`) and `hooks/hooks.json`. It is never propagated to the prompt-file parser. As a consequence, plugins recognized as **Claude-format** but installed via `chat.pluginMarketplaces` or registered via `chat.pluginLocations` have their `rules/*.md` and `agents/*.md` files parsed with **Copilot semantics**, so Claude-only frontmatter fields are silently dropped.

Two visible consequences:

1. **Rules**: the Claude `paths` frontmatter is ignored. Rules with no `applyTo` are then silently skipped by the auto-instructions pipeline (`computeAutomaticInstructions.ts`: `if (!pattern) continue`).
2. **Agents**: `tools: Read, Edit, Bash` and `model: sonnet` are not mapped to Copilot tool/model identifiers, so the agent either fails to invoke its tools or falls back to defaults.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Configure a Claude-format plugin source, e.g.:
   ```jsonc
   "chat.pluginMarketplaces": ["obra/superpowers-marketplace"]
   ```
   or point `chat.pluginLocations` at a local Claude-style plugin folder.
2. Install/enable a plugin laid out as:
   ```
   <plugin>/
     .claude-plugin/plugin.json
     rules/foo.md           # frontmatter: paths: ['**']
     agents/reviewer.md     # frontmatter: tools: Read, Edit; model: sonnet
   ```
3. Open a chat and try to use the agent / observe whether the rule is injected.

## Expected Behavior

Because the plugin was detected as Claude-format, files inside it should be parsed with Claude semantics, equivalent to a hand-placed `.claude/` directory:

- `rules/*.md` with `paths` → `paths` becomes the effective `applyTo` (defaulting to `**` when omitted).
- `agents/*.md` → resolves to `Target.Claude`, so `mapClaudeModels()` and `mapClaudeTools()` run on `model` / `tools`.

## Actual Behavior

Frontmatter transformation is currently gated only by URI path substring, not by plugin format:

- `evaluateApplyToPattern(header, isClaudeRules)` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/promptFileParser.ts` — `isClaudeRules` is true only when `isInClaudeRulesFolder(uri)` matches the literal substring `/.claude/rules/`.
- `getTarget()` in `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/promptSyntax/languageProviders/promptFileAttributes.ts` — returns `Target.Claude` only when `dirname.endsWith('/.claude/agents')`.

Marketplace-installed plugins land in `~/.vscode/agent-plugins/<owner>__<repo>/<plugin>/`, which contains neither substring. Therefore:

- `paths` is dropped, no `applyTo` is computed → the rule is skipped.
- `target` stays `Target.Undefined`, so `mapClaudeModels` / `mapClaudeTools` never run on agent frontmatter.

The same applies to plugins registered via `chat.pluginLocations`.

## Suggested Direction

Propagate the detected plugin format from the plugin scanner down to instruction/agent discovery:

- Carry the plugin format on `IPluginPromptPath` (already known by `agentPluginServiceImpl`).
- In `getInstructionsDiscoveryInfo()`, treat the file as Claude-rules when `isInClaudeRulesFolder(uri) || pluginFormat === 'claude'`.
- In `getTarget()`, return `Target.Claude` for plugin-rooted prompt files when the plugin format is Claude.

This preserves the existing path-based fast paths for `~/.claude/` and gives installed Claude plugins identical behavior to a hand-placed `.claude/` directory.

## Related

- #296617 — preliminary plugin support (Copilot + Claude formats).
- #297148 — install plugins from marketplaces (Copilot + Claude).
- #303051 — broader inconsistency around loading Claude-style customizations from non-`~/.claude` locations (closed without addressing the plugin install path).

- VS Code Version: 1.110.0

Recently (I guess since today's update) the pwsh terminal integration isn't behaving

In my `$Profile` I have the line per the docs

```ps1
if ($env:TERM_PROGRAM -eq "vscode") { . "$(code --locate-shell-integration-path pwsh)" }
```

Opening a pwsh terminal instance triggers an error (in the terminal) and opens a new VSCode instance.

```
    + CategoryInfo          : InvalidOperation: (:String) [], RuntimeException

```

Running `code --locate-shell-integration-path pwsh` outputs nothing and starts an instance of VSCode (from either the integrated terminal or an external pwsh window)

1. `if ($env:TERM_PROGRAM -eq "vscode") { . "$(code --locate-shell-integration-path pwsh)" }` in `$Profile`

body:
Type: Bug This started a few days ago in VS Code version 1.110.0. I have updated to VS Code version 1.111.0 and it still happens. It happens when VS Code is started from a shortcut but not when started from the `code.cmd` file or from `Code.exe` on the command line. I use the VS Code integrated terminal running Powershell (Core) to run git commands on the command line and I have VS code set as my git editor (`core.editor=code --wait`). Normally when I run a command like `git commit` or `git rebase --interactive` a file like `COMMIT_EDITMSG` or `git-rebase-todo` will be opened as a new tab in the existing VS Code window. Very recently, when I run those commands, the file is opened in a separate VS Code window and there are error messages printed to the integrated terminal. When running `git commit --all` there will is a short pause, the message `hint: Waiting for your editor to close the file...` is printed to the integrated terminal, and then the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file is opened in a new VS Code window. After new window opens the following lines are written to the integrated terminal: ``` [46120:0309/104138.853:ERROR:net\disk_cache\cache_util_win.cc:25] Unable to move the cache: Access is denied. (0x5) [46120:0309/104138.853:ERROR:net\disk_cache\disk_cache.cc:236] Unable to create cache [47804:0309/104140.347:ERROR:components\services\storage\service_worker\service_worker_storage.cc:1809] Failed to delete the database: Database IO error ``` The numbers before `:ERROR:` will vary but the parts after `:ERROR:` will always be the same. If I close the new `COMMIT_EDITMSG` window without entering a commit message the `Aborting commit due to empty commit message.` message is written to the integrated terminal. If I do enter a commit message, the commit is created with the specified message. The above describes what happens when I start VS Code from the shortcut in the Windows Start menu's 'All' menu or a shortcut pinned to the taskbar or to the Start Menu. If I start VS code by executing `code` in the Windows Terminal, the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file opens as a new tab and there are no error messages in the integrated terminal. The `code` command will execute `code.cmd` in `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\bin\`. If I start VS Code by executing `AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\Code.exe` in the Windows Terminal, the `COMMIT_EDITMSG` file opens as a new tab and there are no error messages in the integrated terminal. I have tried restarting VS Code, restarting my computer, deleting some folders from `C:\Users\xxxxx\AppData\Roaming\Code` (anything with the word cache) and reinstalling VS Code (without first uninstalling) but none of these have worked. title: GitHub Copilog `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled: false` fills context window in single round.

labels.name: unreleased
labels.description: Patch has not yet been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
In #311196 it was suggested to turn off `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled` in order to allow the user to control when compaction (summarization) occurs. However this results in the context window immediately being filled!

Here's one example:
1. My context window meter shows 48%. I have `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled` set to `true`.
2. I ask the LLM to review the plan. As soon as it starts showing output, the context window meter still shows under 50%.
3. I cancel the review and set `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled` set to `false`. Then I edit and _resubmit the same prompt_.
4. As soon as it starts showing output, **the context window meter jumps to 79%**!

Here is a simpler example:

### With `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled: false`

1. `/compact`
2. Asked LLM to respond to some plan review findings.
3. Meter shows **80%**.

### With `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled: true`

1. `/compact`
2. Asked LLM to respond to the same plan review findings (i.e. the same prompt was given).
3. Meter shows **20%**.

This is with _the exact same prompt_. I realize that LLM responses are nondeterministic. Still nothing explains the _consistent_ enormous difference. `github.copilot.chat.summarizeAgentConversationHistory.enabled: false` will immediately fill the context window in just one or two turns!

```

Commit: 560a9dba96f961efea7b1612916f89e5d5d4d679
```
