title: Add support for local-only tracked changes in Source Control
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! feature_request_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

<!-- Describe the feature you'd like. -->

### **Problem**

Users often maintain local modifications in tracked files that should never be committed, such as:
- local environment configuration
- temporary debug changes
- developer-specific feature flags
- machine-specific adjustments

Current Git solutions like skip-worktree and assume-unchanged are difficult to discover and manage, especially from the VS Code Source Control UI.

Additionally, using .gitignore is not appropriate for tracked files and may introduce repository-wide side effects.

### **Proposed Solution**

Introduce a new SCM resource group named _Local Only_.

Files added to this group would:
- remain modified locally
- be excluded from commits/staging
- avoid repository changes
- avoid modifying .gitignore
- persist only locally for the current workspace/user

This would provide a safer and more discoverable workflow for managing local-only tracked changes directly from the Source Control UI.

### **Example Use Cases**
- local API endpoints
- debug flags
- temporary instrumentation
- developer-specific workspace adjustments
- locally customized settings files
- Related Discussions

### **Related to:**

#313007
#121635

This proposal approaches the problem from a Source Control workflow and UX perspective rather than exposing raw Git internals directly.

### **Additional Context**

I already implemented a working prototype locally, including:
- a dedicated SCM group
- staging prevention
- local persistence behavior
- integration into the Source Control view

title: Remote-SSH server install fails with "extraction path resolves outside root" after #317393

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- Type: Bug -->
<!-- VS Code Version: Insiders, commit 7d7e9513af36a565ce41d60e18972bbc5cad14a2 -->
<!-- OS Version: macOS (client), Linux x86_64 (remote) -->
<!-- Extension: ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh 0.122.0 -->

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Use VS Code Insiders with Remote-SSH to connect to any Linux host where
   `~/.vscode-server-insiders/cli/servers/Insiders-7d7e9513af36a565ce41d60e18972bbc5cad14a2`
   does not yet exist.
2. Let the CLI download and try to extract the server tarball.

### Expected
Server extracts and Remote-SSH connects.

### Actual
Extraction fails with:

```
Error installing server: refusing to write
  /<home>/.vscode-server-insiders/cli/servers/Insiders-<sha>.staging
which resolves to
  /<home>/.vscode-server-insiders/cli/servers/Insiders-<sha>.staging:
extraction path resolves outside root
```

Note the path and its "resolved" form are **identical** — there are no
symlinks involved (verified with `readlink -f` on the full path and
`findmnt` showing plain XFS, no bind mounts). The bug is in OS-agnostic
Rust code (`Path::parent()` + `Path::starts_with`); it does not depend on
the host OS, libc, filesystem type, or symlink presence.

### Root cause

Introduced by #317393. In `cli/src/util/tar.rs` (and the analogous code in
`cli/src/util/zipper.rs`):

```rust
let relative: PathBuf = if skip_first {
    entry_path.iter().skip(1).collect()   // empty PathBuf for bare root-dir entry
} else {
    entry_path.into_owned()
};
let path = safe_extract_join(&canonical_root, &relative)?;
// path == canonical_root when relative is empty

if let Some(p) = path.parent() {           // walks ABOVE the root
    fs::create_dir_all(p)...;
    ensure_canonical_within_root(&canonical_root, p)?;  // fails
}
```

When the tar entry is a bare top-level directory entry (e.g.
`vscode-server-linux-x64/`), `entry_path.iter().skip(1).collect()` yields
an empty `PathBuf`. `safe_extract_join(root, "")` returns `root` itself,
then `path.parent()` walks to the `.staging` directory which is
legitimately outside `canonical_root` (the root is `<staging>/server/`,
set in `ServerBuilder::setup` at `cli/src/tunnels/code_server.rs`).

The error message is doubly confusing because canonicalization is a no-op
on systems without symlinks, so both sides of "which resolves to" print
the same path — but the rejected path is the *parent* of the root, not
the staging path itself.

This affects every install on hosts where the server tarball contains a
bare top-level directory entry, which is the default `tar` produces.

### Proposed fix

Three changes:

**`cli/src/util/extract_safety.rs`** — reject empty entries at the API
boundary, and add a helper that permits equality with the root:

```rust
pub fn safe_extract_join(root_canonical: &Path, entry: &Path)
    -> Result<PathBuf, WrappedError>
{
    if entry.as_os_str().is_empty() {
        return Err(wrap("empty extraction entry",
            "refusing to extract empty archive entry".to_string()));
    }
    // ... existing logic ...
}

pub fn ensure_canonical_within_or_equal_root(
    root_canonical: &Path, path: &Path,
) -> Result<(), WrappedError> {
    let canon = fs::canonicalize(path).map_err(|e| wrap(e,
        format!("could not canonicalize extraction path {}", path.display())))?;
    if canon != root_canonical && !canon.starts_with(root_canonical) {
        return Err(wrap("extraction path resolves outside root",
            format!("refusing to write {} which resolves to {}",
                path.display(), canon.display())));
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

**`cli/src/util/tar.rs` and `cli/src/util/zipper.rs`** — use the
equal-or-within helper for the parent-dir check:

```rust
if let Some(p) = path.parent() {
    fs::create_dir_all(p)...;
    ensure_canonical_within_or_equal_root(&canonical_root, p)?;
}
```

### Test coverage gap

PR #317393's tests only exercise the helpers in isolation with non-empty
paths. None of the following existed:

- A test passing an empty `PathBuf` to `safe_extract_join`.
- A test passing the root itself to the containment check.
- Any integration test that runs `decompress_tarball` / `unzip_file`
  end-to-end against a real synthetic archive.

`cli/src/util/tar.rs` and `cli/src/util/zipper.rs` have **zero tests**.

Suggested additions:

```rust
// extract_safety.rs
#[test]
fn safe_extract_join_rejects_empty_relative() {
    let root = fs::canonicalize(std::env::temp_dir()).unwrap();
    assert!(safe_extract_join(&root, Path::new("")).is_err());
}

#[test]
fn ensure_canonical_within_or_equal_root_allows_root_itself() {
    let root = fs::canonicalize(std::env::temp_dir()).unwrap();
    assert!(ensure_canonical_within_or_equal_root(&root, &root).is_ok());
}

// tar.rs (new #[cfg(test)] mod) — synthesize a tarball with a bare
// top-level directory entry "prefix/" + "prefix/file.txt", run
// decompress_tarball, assert success and that file.txt was written.
```

### Workarounds
- `"remote.SSH.useExecServer": false` in user settings (uses legacy path).
- Use VS Code Stable until fixed.

Happy to open a PR with the patch + tests above if it's useful.

title: Devcontainer setup ad-hoc parsing fails on Rocky 8.5

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: linux
labels.description: Issues with VS Code on Linux

labels.name: good first issue
labels.description: Issues identified as good for first-time contributors

labels.name: remote
labels.description: Remote system operations issues

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.92.0 (Commit: b1c0a14de1414fcdaa400695b4db1c0799bc3124)
- OS Version: Rocky Linux 8.5

The `check-requirements-linux.sh` script that runs as part of a devcontainer setup wants to parse the OS version from `/etc/os-release`.
That file "is a newline-separated list of environment-like shell-compatible variable assignments" (see `man os-relase` on Linux).
The script tries to parse the value it wants [here](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/resources/server/bin/helpers/check-requirements-linux.sh#L34), by running:

```
OS_ID="$(cat /etc/os-release | grep -Eo 'ID=([^"]+)' | sed -n '1s/ID=//p')"
```

This looks for a key-value pair of the form `ID=value` and strips out the `ID=` part.
It assumes the pair is not of the form `ID="value"`, which appears to be permitted by the file format, and appears in the wild in Rocky 8.5 where the relevant line reads:

```
ID="rocky"
```

In that case the `grep` command will fail to match the line and the value of `OS_ID` will be empty, resulting in a non-zero exit code of 1.

This could be fixed by changing the variable assignment to:

```
OS_ID=$(source /etc/os-release && echo $ID)
```

This will run a subshell in which the values in the file get assigned to environment variables, which removes any surrounding `"` marks.
Since this runs in a subshell the current shell's environment will not pick up these variables.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Start a devcontainer built from Rocky 8.5.

title: Terminal tool zsh failures

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: chat-terminal
labels.description: The run in terminal tool in chat

body:
Here's a list of terminal tool failures in my chat sessions from writing bad zsh commands, because the model isn't trained on working in a real shell environment.

Most common ones that I've noticed seeing:
- using `!` incorrectly, eg `echo "hello!"` is an incorrect use of `!`, and this will hang on `dquote>`
- `zsh: command not found: #`
- Incorrect use of `==`, eg:

```
## Request
~~~
id   : toolu_vrtx_01SWtUxMk2tNJW69APCybBo6__vscode-1778605228075
tool : run_in_terminal
args : {
  "command": "cat .planning/config.json 2>/dev/null; echo ===; git log --oneline 4047ede..HEAD | wc -l; git log --format='%ai' 4047ede | tail -1; git log --format='%ai' HEAD | head -1; git diff --stat 4047ede..HEAD | tail -1; find src tests -name '*.ts' | xargs wc -l | tail -1",
  "explanation": "Gather stats",
  "goal": "Stats",
  "mode": "sync"
}
~~~
## Response
~~~
{
  "mode": "yolo",
  "granularity": "coarse",
  "parallelization": true,
  "commit_docs": true,
  "model_profile": "quality",
  "git": {
    "branching_strategy": "phase",
    "phase_branch_template": "phase-{phase}-{slug}"
  },
  "workflow": {
    "research": true,
    "plan_check": true,
    "verifier": true,
    "nyquist_validation": true
  }
}zsh: == not found
```
Full list that were detected in my vscode chat sessions:

| Category                             | Pattern                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Bad substitution                     | JS/template/string snippets in double quotes caused zsh expansion, e.g. `zsh: bad substitution`.                                                                               |
| Comment lines executed               | `# ...` lines were sent to interactive zsh, producing `zsh: command not found: #`; comments with parentheses produced `unknown file attribute` / `invalid mode specification`. |
| Unmatched globs                      | zsh `nomatch` turned missing globs into failures before redirection, e.g. `zsh: no matches found: *.md`.                                                                       |
| Read-only `status` variable          | Commands used `status=$?`; zsh has `status` as a read-only special parameter.                                                                                                  |
| Bare `==` / `===` separators         | `echo ===`, `echo ==== $f`, `echo ===TAIL===` triggered zsh equals expansion, e.g. `zsh: === not found`.                                                                       |
| Multiword command in scalar variable | `DAP="node path/to/file.js"; $DAP ...` failed because zsh does not split scalar variables like bash.                                                                           |
| Missing `timeout`                    | GNU `timeout` assumed on macOS: `zsh: command not found: timeout`.                                                                                                             |
| Bad timeout substitute               | `perl -e 'alarm 3; exec @ARGV' ...` was attempted but produced `zsh: alarm perl ...`.                                                                                          |
| Argument list too long               | Huge JSON/string args sent inline: `zsh: argument list too long: node`.                                                                                                        |

title: Support variables when resolving values in settings

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: config
labels.description: VS Code configuration, settings

milestone.title: Backlog
milestone.description: Work not yet planned for a specific release.

body:
Hi,

I was just reading the [latest updates](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates) and it says one can install `typescript@next` globally and then set `typescript.tsdk` so VS Code can use the appropriate version/installation. In a team environment, I'd like to put that setting in our project, something like:

`.vscode/settings.json`:

``` json
{
    "typescript.tsdk": "%APPDATA%/npm/node_modules/typescript/lib"
}
```

The problem is restarting VS Code results in an error:

```
The path c:\Projects\Derp\%APPDATA%\npm\node_modules\typescript\lib doesn't point to a valid tsserver install. TypeScript language features will be disabled.
```

Now the setting needs to be per-person because I highly doubt my teammates have `tsc` installed in `C:\Users\Olson\AppData\Roaming\npm\node_modules\typescript\lib` :wink:

Could we get environment variables evaluated on that and all other settings that involve paths?

I haven't tested other paths, but I see these in the Default Settings:
- `git.path`
- `markdown.styles`
- `json.schemas`
- `typescript.tsdk`
- `php.validate.executablePath`

title: Subagent detection in responsesApi.ts is broken (startsWith('subagent') never matches)
body:
## Summary

The subagent gate in `responsesApi.ts` uses `subType?.startsWith('subagent')`, which never matches in practice. Producers set `subType` to `'execution_subagent'` and `'search_subagent'`, so the check evaluates to `false` for every real subagent request.

## Where

`extensions/copilot/src/platform/endpoint/node/responsesApi.ts:69`

```ts
const isAllowedConversationAgent = options.location === ChatLocation.Agent || options.location === ChatLocation.MessagesProxy;
const isSubagent = options.telemetryProperties?.subType?.startsWith('subagent') ?? false;
const shouldDeferTools = toolSearchEnabled && isAllowedConversationAgent && !isSubagent;
```

## Why it's wrong

The producers emit:

| File | Value |
|---|---|
| `extension/prompt/node/executionSubagentToolCallingLoop.ts:366` | `'execution_subagent'` |
| `extension/prompt/node/searchSubagentToolCallingLoop.ts:172` | `'search_subagent'` |

Commit `5e01ed5de06` (*"rename agent type so it doesn't get redacted"*) renamed the values from `subagent/execution` and `subagent/search` to put `subagent` as a suffix rather than a prefix. The detection in `responsesApi.ts` was not updated to match, so `shouldDeferTools` ends up `true` for subagent runs even though the gate is supposed to exclude them.

## Impact

Client-executed tool search defers tools for subagent requests today, contrary to the comment / gate intent. Behavior may or may not be desirable — needs a product call — but at minimum the code does not match the apparent design.

## Suggested fix

```ts
const isSubagent = options.telemetryProperties?.subType?.includes('subagent') ?? false;
```

`includes` matches both current names (`execution_subagent`, `search_subagent`) and stays robust if new subagent types like `explore_subagent` are added later. Same fix was applied to the new extended-cache-ttl gate in `messagesApi.ts` (PR / branch `dev/bhavyau/anthropic-extended-cache-ttl`).

Optionally, extract a tiny shared helper near `subType?: string` in `extensions/copilot/src/platform/networking/common/networking.ts` so both API paths stay in sync.

## Acceptance

- [ ] `responsesApi.ts` correctly identifies `execution_subagent` and `search_subagent` requests as subagents
- [ ] (Optional) Shared helper used by both Messages and Responses API code paths

cc @bhavyaus — found while implementing the Anthropic extended cache TTL gate.

title: Recently Opened: deleted remote (WSL/SSH) folders are never removed from the list

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: WSL
labels.description: Issue when using WSL

labels.name: remote
labels.description: Remote system operations issues

labels.name: workbench-history
labels.description: History item issues

body:
<!-- Type: Bug / Feature request -->

## Summary

Deleted **remote** folders (e.g. opened via Remote - WSL) are never removed from **File → Open Recent**, even after you attempt to open them and the open fails because the path no longer exists. They stay in the top "workspaces/folders" section indefinitely.

This is distinct from #74465 (closed by-design in 2019). There, the resolution was:

> "You can have many folders in your recents and we shouldn't hit the disk many times for each one when you open a vscode window. So instead if you try to open it and we find that it doesn't exist anymore, then we remove it from the list."

That remove-on-open cleanup works for local folders, but it does **not** happen for remote (`vscode-remote://wsl+...`) folders - so a deleted WSL workspace lingers forever. I am **not** asking for a disk scan of every recent entry on startup (that's the perf concern from #74465); I'm reporting that the existing "remove it when we find it doesn't exist on open" behavior is missing for the remote case.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. In WSL, create a folder: `mkdir ~/ghost-test`
2. Open it in VS Code via Remote - WSL (`code ~/ghost-test` from the WSL shell).
3. Close the window.
4. Delete the folder in WSL: `rm -rf ~/ghost-test`
5. Open VS Code and check **File → Open Recent**.

## Expected

After attempting to open the now-missing remote folder (and VS Code finding it doesn't exist), the entry is removed from Open Recent — the same behavior #74465 says already happens for local folders.

## Actual

The entry `vscode-remote://wsl+<distro>/home/<user>/ghost-test` remains in Open Recent (top section) permanently. Attempting to open it surfaces an error, but the stale entry is **not** removed, so it keeps polluting the recent list and the `> Open Recent` quick-pick autocomplete.

## Notes

- The entry persists across restarts; the only way to remove it via "Open Recent > More ..." and then clicking "X".
- Same problem is likely to affect other remote authorities (Remote - SSH, Dev Containers, Tunnels), since they share the remove-on-open path.

## VS Code version

- Version: 1.121.0 (stable), commit f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282
- Remote: Remote - WSL (server build same version)
- OS: Windows host + WSL2 (Ubuntu)

Related: #74465, #55367

title: Agents: Steering brings me to the new session screen
body: I only repro in the Agents window and not a VS Code editor window with the Copilot CLI: - Open Agents window - Kick off a message to Copilot CLI, i.e. `Update my site's theming to be green instead of blue` - Send message - As session starts, steer the session, i.e. `Actually make it purple instead` - ❓ I'm brought to the new session screen

title: Remote `ssh` connection fails from Debian -> MacOS

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: important
labels.description: Issue identified as high-priority

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
Type: Bug New in this release; was working before. To Reproduce ============ 0. This assumes both systems are set up for passwordless `ssh`, including loading a certificate locally with `ssh-add`. Further it assumes `VSCode` has a previous definition of the desired remote `ssh` connection. I don't know if that's essential (as opposed to creating it on the fly) but it might be. It could well be essential that the connection was used with the immediately preceding released version of `VSCode`. 1. Launch `VSCode` 1.121 from a Debian 11 (old) GNU/Linux x64 system. 2. Use a regular terminal to `ssh` to the remote system and run `caffeinate -t 12000` to keep it from idling and dropping the connection, which has been a problem. Leave the session connected and do nothing more in that terminal. 3. Use an already defined remote `ssh` session in `VSCode` to connect to another machine running MacOS 26.5, M1 chip. The `VSCode` server has *not* been upgraded to 1.121 AFAIK, and so the server is presumably running 1.120 until that happens. 4. The remote machine also has a regular GUI installation of `VSCode`. That has been upgraded to 1.121. I've been told that's independent of the server installation. Actual Result =========== The local `VSCode`'s `Remote - SSH` Output reports, after preliminaries, ``` [23:28:11.462] Failed to parse remote port from server output [23:28:11.463] Terminating local server [23:28:11.466] Resolver error: Error at y.Create (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:745163) at t.handleInstallOutput (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:743244) at e (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:799508) at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:103:5) at async /home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:824766 at async t.withShowDetailsEvent (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:828461) at async /home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:796220 at async P (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:794275) at async t.resolveWithLocalServer (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:795772) at async A (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:821567) at async t.resolve (/home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:825858) at async /home/ross/.vscode/extensions/ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh-0.122.0/out/extension.js:2:1117225 [23:28:11.484] ------ [23:28:11.486] No hints found in the recent session. [23:28:11.491] Local server exit: 15 ``` The remote connection icon on the bottom left of the outer frame for `VSCode` shows status of `Disconnected`. Which is a little odd, considering it was clearly able to talk to the remote machine for awhile. Expected Result ============= Since this is the first contact with this version, I would expect `Remote - SSH` to report downloading new components and then successfully connecting, ready to work. Speculation ========== 1. The remote server is running v 1.120 while the local client is 1.121. They don't know how to talk to each other. And, since they can't, the local client can't get the server to install a new version of the server. 2. 1.121 either changed the timeout logic or interval; downloading the new components takes longer than this allows, hence the failure. Additional Transcript ================= Before the section quoted above,`Remote - SSH` reports what look to me like fairly normal preliminaries: ``` [23:28:09.774] Log Level: 2 [23:28:09.864] SSH title: Per-turn agent filtering busts the system prompt cache

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Symptom

Across two consecutive chat requests in the same session, the entire ~9.7k-token system block is byte-identical except for a single 9-character substring inside the `<agents>` listing:

```diff
@@ -544,3 +544,3 @@
 <agent>
-<name>Plan</name>
+<name>Ask</name>
 </agent>
```

The system-level prompt cache breakpoint sits at the end of the system block, so this single-line mutation invalidates the entire system prefix. The model re-pays the cost of caching ~9.7k tokens of otherwise-stable instructions on every turn that flips the active mode.

<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->

In the latest version of VSCode it is now possible to use local models (for example ollama) in the copilot (https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/copilot/language-models#_bring-your-own-language-model-key). However it still requires to login to GitHub to get started. Can this limitation be removed to allow use of copilot with local model completely offline?

title: Calling vscode.lm.selectChatModels inside an extension results in resource spikes in Remote-SSH scenarios with Copilot Chat extension kind set to "ui"
body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.122.0
- OS Version: Ubuntu 22.04
- Copilot extension version: 1.388.0 

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a simple extension, based on the hello-world example extension, that uses the `vscode.lm.selectChatModels` API. The `extension.ts` file can look like this:

```
import * as vscode from 'vscode';

export function activate(context: vscode.ExtensionContext) {

	const disposable = vscode.commands.registerCommand('extension.helloWorld', async () => {
		await vscode.lm.selectChatModels({ vendor: 'copilot' });
	});

	context.subscriptions.push(disposable);
}
```
2. Set the extension kind for `github.copilot-chat` to "ui"
3. Connect through Remote-SSH to a machine and install the dummy extension on that machine 
4. Run the command that triggers the selectChatModels call

Result:

The `Extension Host (Remote)` console is flooded with warnings like these:
```
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gpt-4.1' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/oswe-vscode-prime' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gpt-5.2' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/auto' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/copilot-utility-small' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.672 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/copilot-utility' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/claude-sonnet-4.6' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gemini-3.1-pro-preview' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
2026-05-28 13:33:51.673 [warning] [LanguageModelProxy](test.helloworld-sample) Could not find model 'copilot/gemini-3.5-flash' in local cache. Trying to resolve model again.
```
As you can see, these are fired non-stop (thousands each second), and the resource usage of the local machine spikes.

If I remove "github.copilot-chat": "ui" from the value of the remote.extensionKind setting, these warnings are no longer triggered.

title: `@vscode/sandbox-runtime` bwrap sandboxing incompatible with devcontainers (non-root Docker users have CapEff=0)

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
VS Code version: Insiders (May 2026)

# Summary:
Agent mode tool calls are now wrapped in a bwrap sandbox via @vscode/sandbox-runtime. This silently breaks in devcontainer environments where the VS Code session runs as a non-root user (e.g. vscode), causing all agent tool calls to fail with bwrap: capset failed: Operation not permitted.

# Root cause:
Docker containers running as non-root have zero effective capabilities (CapEff: 0). bwrap's child namespace setup requires cap_setpcap in the effective set to call prctl(PR_CAPBSET_DROP) — this is impossible to grant to non-root users inside Docker regardless of cap_add, setcap, or seccomp/AppArmor settings.

@vscode/sandbox-runtime already has an enableWeakerNestedSandbox config flag described as "for Docker environments", but it is not automatically detected or applied.

# Expected behavior:
VS Code should detect it is running inside a Docker container (e.g. via .dockerenv, cgroup markers, or container env vars) and either:

- automatically enable enableWeakerNestedSandbox, or
- disable bwrap sandboxing entirely and surface a warning

# Workaround:
Grant vscode passwordless sudo access to bwrap only, and shadow it with a wrapper script — a significant security/ergonomics burden that should not fall on devcontainer users.

# Impact:

All Copilot agent users using devcontainers on VS Code Insiders are silently broken.

title: Extension host crash loop (exit code 5) on startup when Copilot Chat is enabled in a large multi-root workspace

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: freeze-slow-crash-leak
labels.description: VS Code crashing, performance, freeze and memory leak issues

labels.name: git
labels.description: GIT issues

body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: `github.copilot-chat@0.48.1`
- VS Code Version: 1.120.0 (commit 0958016b2af9f09bb4257e0df4a95e2f90590f9f, arm64)
- OS Version: macOS 26.2 (Darwin arm64)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): N/A, the host crashes at startup before any chat session is opened
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): N/A, see above
- Logs: see below

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open a multi-root `.code-workspace` containing ~50 folders (mix of git repos, several large; same workspace as microsoft/vscode#318279).
2. Have `github.copilot-chat@0.48.1` enabled. Do not interact with the window.
3. Wait ~25 to 30 seconds after the Extension Host starts.
4. Renderer reports `Extension host (LocalProcess pid: N) terminated unexpectedly`; main log shows `exit code: 5, signal: unknown`. Auto-restart starts a new host, which dies on the same cycle.

## Bisect

Reproduced with `code --disable-extension <id>` per launch, watching `~/Library/Application Support/Code/logs/<ts>/main.log` for ~75s after each Extension Host start:

| Launch | What was disabled (active 3rd-party set) | Outcome |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `--disable-extensions` | all 3rd-party | survive >80s |
| Round 1 | second half of activated extensions, alphabetical | crash ~26s |
| Round 2 | + claude-code, copilot-chat, postgres, prettier, biome | survive >80s |
| Round 3 | + only prettier, biome | crash ~21s |
| Round 4 | + postgres also | crash ~21s |
| Round 5 | + copilot-chat also | survive >80s |
| Re-enable all | (none, copilot-chat back on) | crash ~28s |

Disabling `github.copilot-chat` alone with every other extension enabled is sufficient. Re-enabling it brings the loop back.

## Logs (one cycle)

`main.log`:

```
2026-05-28 10:04:46.787 [info] Extension host with pid 40087 exited with code: 5, signal: unknown.
2026-05-28 10:04:46.787 [error] [UtilityProcess id: 2, type: extensionHost, pid: 40087]: crashed with code 5 and reason 'crashed'
```

`renderer.log`:

```
2026-05-28 10:04:19.398 [info] Started local extension host with pid 40087.
2026-05-28 10:04:46.790 [error] Extension host (LocalProcess pid: 40087) terminated unexpectedly. The following extensions were running: ..., GitHub.copilot-chat, ...
```

`exthost.log` (next cycle, right before next kill, only chat-related error):

```
2026-05-28 10:04:54.643 [error] Error calling provideChatSessionProviderOptions for handle 0: Canceled
```

Earlier the same day, a same-coalition Homebrew `node` child (thread named `chrome-devtools-mcp`) crashed with the classic V8 OOM stack (`node::OOMErrorHandler` to `v8::internal::V8::FatalProcessOutOfMemory`). Different process, but consistent memory-pressure signal in this VS Code instance, suggesting the exthost is also V8-OOMing.

## Possibly related

microsoft/vscode-copilot-release#14139 is also `exit code 5` in a Copilot Chat workflow on macOS, but that one is the *renderer* crashing during sustained Remote-SSH chat usage; this one is the *local extension host* crashing on startup. Likely a shared memory-pressure root cause.

## Workaround

Disable `github.copilot-chat`, per launch with `code --disable-extension github.copilot-chat`, or persistently via the Extensions sidebar.

title: BYOK: Terminal steering request may not reuse frozen customizations-index

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- Please search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates -->
<!-- Please attach logs to help us diagnose your issue -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Stable/ Insiders / built from current `main` around #316191 / #316466 / #316478
- VS Code Version: Stable 1.121.0(f6cfa2ea2403534de03f069bdf160d06451ed282) / Insiders 1.122.0(b51c2a1a37574dec35d0426b2ee9c3538c9774f4)
- OS Version: macOS Tahoe 26.4
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): agent mode, terminal steering request
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): DeepSeek V4 Pro via LM provider
- Logs: provider-side request dump; summarized below

Possible follow-up to #316191 / #316466.
I searched existing issues. The closest one I found is #301966, but that was about auto-compaction dropping instructions/skills/agents. This report is specifically about system-initiated terminal completion steering requests.

With `github.copilot.chat.freezeCustomizationsIndex=true`, normal agent turns keep the frozen customizations-index in the system prompt. But a system-initiated terminal completion steering request appears to lose the `<skills>` / `<agents>` block before it reaches the LM provider.

Observed from provider-side dumps:

- normal agent turn: system prompt is ~23.5k chars / 295 lines and includes `<instructions>`, `<skills>`, `<agents>`
- terminal completion steering turn: request starts with `[Terminal <id> notification: command completed ...]`; system prompt is ~9.2k chars / 92 lines and includes base agent instructions, but no `<skills>` / `<agents>`

Source reading suggests terminal completion notifications are sent from `RunInTerminalTool` with `queue: ChatRequestQueueKind.Steering` and `isSystemInitiated: true`. That path seems to inherit `userSelectedModelId`, `modeInfo`, and `userSelectedTools`, but not `instructionContext`.

Since automatic instructions collection in `chatServiceImpl` depends on `options.instructionContext`, the steering request may not get a live customizations-index variable. In my dump, the frozen customizations-index was not applied either.

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Enable `github.copilot.chat.freezeCustomizationsIndex`.
2. Start a long agent conversation where normal agent requests include `<instructions>`, `<skills>`, and `<agents>` in the system prompt.
3. Have the agent run a terminal command that continues in the background and later completes.
4. Inspect the LM provider request for the terminal completion steering turn.

Expected:

The terminal steering request either reuses the conversation’s frozen customizations-index, or this is documented/intentional so providers can treat it as a separate cache scope.

Actual:

The terminal completion steering request appears to use the agent path, but its system prompt omits `<skills>` / `<agents>`, causing a different system prompt cache prefix.

Question:

Should system-initiated terminal steering requests reuse the frozen customizations-index from the conversation, or are they intentionally outside the cache-stability scope of #316191?

cc @bhavyaus since this seems related to `freezeCustomizationsIndex`, but I’m not sure whether this is expected behavior or a remaining edge case.

title: Extension host repeatedly killed in large multi-root workspaces (vscode.git decoration storm)

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: freeze-slow-crash-leak
labels.description: VS Code crashing, performance, freeze and memory leak issues

labels.name: git
labels.description: GIT issues

labels.name: unreleased
labels.description: Patch has not yet been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.120.0
- OS Version: macOS 26.3.1 (darwin-arm64, Darwin 25.3.0)

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Create a multi-root `.code-workspace` containing around 50 folders, most of them git repos of varying sizes (small repos, larger codebases with Python venvs, a clone of microsoft/vscode).
2. Open it and wait. Don't open files or interact.

The extension host gets killed about 30 seconds after starting, auto-restarts, and dies again on the same cycle. `renderer.log` shows `Extension host (LocalProcess pid: N) terminated unexpectedly` with no stack and no signal. `exthost.log` shows several thousand `[Decorations] CAPPING events from decorations provider vscode.git` warnings firing in tight succession just before each kill. Cadence matches the IPC unresponsive watcher killing after missed pings.

Bisecting by halving the folder list and swapping folders at the boundary showed the trigger is aggregate vscode.git scan load, not any specific folder. Above a workspace-size threshold the host can't survive its own startup scan.

Ruled out:

- Extensions. Same crash with `--disable-extensions`.
- Folder identity. Removing the largest folder (microsoft/vscode clone), the most recently added folders, or individual suspect folders had no effect; only total count matters.
- File-watcher noise. Adding `**/.venv/**`, `**/__pycache__/**`, `**/.pytest_cache/**` to `files.watcherExclude` changed nothing.
- `git.autoRepositoryDetection: "openEditors"`. No effect, since explicit workspace folders still get scanned.

Workarounds:

- `git.enabled: false` at workspace level stops it (loses Source Control panel and decorations).
- Splitting the workspace and dropping enough folders to get below the threshold.

My workspace grew gradually, so I can't say if this is a recent regression or a longstanding threshold I just crossed. Either way, vscode.git's startup scan starves the host instead of degrading gracefully.

`renderer.log` (one cycle):
```
2026-05-25 21:14:15.585 [info] Started local extension host with pid 98481.
2026-05-25 21:14:35.663 [error] Extension host (LocalProcess pid: 98481) terminated unexpectedly. The following extensions were running: vscode.github-authentication, vscode.emmet, vscode.ipynb, vscode.git-base, vscode.npm, vscode.git, vscode.github, GitHub.copilot-chat, vscode.debug-auto-launch, vscode.merge-conflict, vscode.configuration-editing, vscode.json-language-features, vscode.typescript-language-features, vscode.terminal-suggest, vscode.microsoft-authentication, vscode.extension-editing
2026-05-25 21:14:35.697 [info] Started local extension host with pid 9281.
```

`exthost.log` (CAPPING warnings before the kill):
```
2026-05-25 21:14:21.950 [warning] [Decorations] CAPPING events from decorations provider vscode.git 330
2026-05-25 21:14:24.356 [warning] [Decorations] CAPPING events from decorations provider vscode.git 8585
2026-05-25 21:14:24.411 [warning] [Decorations] CAPPING events from decorations provider vscode.git 8585
2026-05-25 21:14:24.741 [warning] [Decorations] CAPPING events from decorations provider vscode.git 8585
```

title: sessions: move IAgentHostFilterService out of providers layer

labels.name: debt
labels.description: Code quality issues

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Architecture context

The `src/vs/sessions/contrib/` folder is organized into two areas:

- **`contrib/providers/`** — Session provider implementations (e.g. `copilotChatSessions`, `remoteAgentHost`, `agentHost`). These are isolated backend implementations that should not be imported by other contrib folders.
- **`contrib/<feature>/`** — Feature contributions (e.g. `chat`, `changes`, `sessions`). These implement UI features and may depend on services but must **not** import directly from `contrib/providers/`.

The dependency direction is **providers → sessions workbench**, not the other way around. Providers depend on the sessions workbench services and APIs to register themselves and contribute UI. The sessions workbench must never depend on provider internals.

An ESLint layer rule (`contrib/providers/*/~` before `contrib/*/~` in `eslint.config.js`) enforces this boundary. See `src/vs/sessions/LAYERS.md` for the full layering rules.

## Problem

`IAgentHostFilterService` is defined in `contrib/providers/remoteAgentHost/` but consumed by three non-provider feature contributions, violating the layer rule. Each import currently has an `eslint-disable-next-line` suppression:

| File | Line |
|------|------|
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/newChatViewPane.ts` | 33 |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/chat/browser/webWorkspacePicker.ts` | 23 |
| `src/vs/sessions/contrib/sessions/browser/views/sessionsList.ts` | 48 |

## Guidance

Provider-specific information — including anything from agent host — must not leak into the sessions workbench layer. All information should flow through the session provider interfaces (`ISessionsProvider`, `ISessionsManagementService`) or via menu/command contributions. If a feature contribution needs data that currently lives in a provider, the provider should expose it through the existing session provider contract or contribute UI via menus, not through direct imports.

**Important:** Do not add provider-specific concepts to `ISessionsProvider` or `ISessionsManagementService` to work around this. Only extend those interfaces with functionality that is genuinely generic and applicable to all providers.

title: BYOK Responses retry for previous_response_not_found re-sends previous_response_id because OpenAIEndpoint overwrites ignoreStatefulMarker

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
Type: Bug ### Environment - VS Code Insiders - Built-in Copilot extension bundled with Insiders - BYOK OpenAI-compatible endpoint - Responses API (Codex CLI Backend) - Reproduced against a local OpenAI-compatible test server ### Summary When a BYOK Responses request fails with HTTP 400: ```json { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx.", "error": { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx." } } ``` VS Code appears to retry, but the retry still includes the same `previous_response_id`. Expected behavior is to retry once **without** the stateful marker / `previous_response_id`. ### Expected behavior After receiving HTTP 400 with root-level: - `code: "previous_response_not_found"` - `message: "No response found for previous_response_id ..."` the client should classify the failure as an invalid stateful marker and retry once without `previous_response_id`. ### Actual behavior A second `POST /v1/responses` is sent, but it still contains the same `previous_response_id`, so the request fails again with the same 400 and the user sees an error instead of recovery. ### Repro 1. Configure Copilot BYOK against an OpenAI-compatible Responses endpoint. 2. Start a stateful conversation so the client sends `previous_response_id`. 3. Make the server return HTTP 400 with: ```json { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx.", "error": { "code": "previous_response_not_found", "message": "No response found for previous_response_id resp_xxx." } } ``` 4. Observe that VS Code sends a second request. 5. Observe that the second request still contains the same `previous_response_id`. ### Notes I first suspected the server payload shape, but I verified the live server already returns root-level `code` and `message` as required. The remaining issue is that the retry request still includes the original `previous_response_id`. ### Evidence The request log shows: - first `POST /v1/responses` with `previous_response_id = X` - HTTP 400 - second `POST /v1/responses` also with `previous_response_id = X` - HTTP 400 again So the retry is happening, but the stateful marker is not being cleared. ### Code analysis The retry path appears to be intended to work: - `extensions/copilot/src/extension/prompt/node/chatMLFetcher.ts` - recognizes HTTP 400 with `jsonData.code === 'previous_response_not_found'` - `extensions/copilot/src/platform/endpoint/node/chatEndpoint.ts` - converts that into `InvalidStatefulMarker` - retries with `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` However, the BYOK `OpenAIEndpoint` appears to override that retry flag. #### 1) `createRequestBody()` mutates `ignoreStatefulMarker` `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts` ```ts const zdr = !!this.modelMetadata.zeroDataRetentionEnabled; options.ignoreStatefulMarker = zdr; const body = super.createRequestBody(options); ``` This overwrites `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` during retry back to `false` in the normal non-ZDR case. #### 2) `makeChatRequest2()` also forces `ignoreStatefulMarker: false` `extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts` ```ts const modifiedOptions: IMakeChatRequestOptions = { ...options, ignoreStatefulMarker: false }; const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(modifiedOptions, token); ``` Even if this is not the primary reason for the observed retry failure, it is also suspicious because it prevents upstream intent from being preserved. ### Proposed patch I believe the BYOK endpoint should preserve an existing retry flag instead of overwriting it. ```diff diff --git a/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts b/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts index XXXXXXX..YYYYYYY 100644 --- a/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts +++ b/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts @@ -240,7 +240,7 @@ export class OpenAIEndpoint extends ChatEndpoint { if (this.useResponsesApi) { // Handle Responses API: customize the body directly const zdr = !!this.modelMetadata.zeroDataRetentionEnabled; - options.ignoreStatefulMarker = zdr; + options.ignoreStatefulMarker = !!options.ignoreStatefulMarker || zdr; const body = super.createRequestBody(options); body.store = !zdr; body.n = undefined; @@ -367,10 +367,8 @@ export class OpenAIEndpoint extends ChatEndpoint { } public override async makeChatRequest2(options: IMakeChatRequestOptions, token: CancellationToken): Promise { - // Apply ignoreStatefulMarker: false for initial request - const modifiedOptions: IMakeChatRequestOptions = { ...options, ignoreStatefulMarker: false }; - const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(modifiedOptions, token); - return hydrateBYOKErrorMessages(response); + const response = await super.makeChatRequest2(options, token); + return hydrateBYOKErrorMessages(response); } } ``` ### Why this patch - `ChatEndpoint.makeChatRequest2()` already computes the default behavior for `ignoreStatefulMarker`. - A retry from `InvalidStatefulMarker` should be allowed to preserve `ignoreStatefulMarker: true`. - ZDR should still disable stateful marker reuse, but it should not erase an already-true retry flag. ### Suggested regression tests Please consider adding a BYOK regression test covering this flow: 1. initial Responses request uses a stateful marker 2. server returns HTTP 400 `previous_response_not_found` 3. retry is triggered 4. retry request body omits `previous_response_id` A targeted test around `OpenAIEndpoint.createRequestBody()` preserving `ignoreStatefulMarker: true` would also help. VS Code version: Code - Insiders 1.121.0-insider (f805d63defafb2ae798360714d6e7600d154a6df, 2026-05-15T12:52:51-04:00) OS version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200 Modes:

title: Allow sending chat requests with attachments only

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: verification-needed
labels.description: Verification of issue is requested

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body: When using chat, attaching an image or file currently requires typing text before the request can be sent. This makes attachment-only prompts awkward, especially for screenshots or files where the attachment itself is the complete request context. Expected behavior: - Users can send a chat request with only explicit attachments, such as images, files, etc. - The UI should not render an empty-looking request. - For attachment-only requests, the user-visible request text should be a localized summary such as: - `Attached 1 image` - `Attached 2 images` - `Attached 1 file` - `Attached 2 files` - Or find a better way to do that Attached photo: attachment selected, but send is blocked until text is typed.

title: Copilot OTel: gen_ai.request.model and gen_ai.response.model use inconsistent naming formats

labels.name: unreleased
labels.description: Patch has not yet been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Summary

On `chat` and `invoke_agent` spans emitted by `copilot-chat`, the `gen_ai.request.model` and `gen_ai.response.model` attributes use different naming formats for the same logical model, causing deduplication/filtering issues for telemetry consumers.

## Observed data

| `gen_ai.request.model` | `gen_ai.response.model` | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| `claude-opus-4.6` | `claude-opus-4-6` | Dot → hyphen version separator |
| `gpt-5.4-mini` | `gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17` | Alias → dated snapshot (expected) |
| `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` | `gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18` | ✅ Consistent |

## Impact

- `GROUP BY` / `DISTINCT` on model produce duplicate rows for Claude
- Filtering by one format misses spans recorded with the other
- The `gpt-5.4-mini` → `gpt-5.4-mini-2026-03-17` case is arguably correct (alias → resolved), but `claude-opus-4.6` → `claude-opus-4-6` is purely a formatting inconsistency

## Expected

Per [OTel GenAI semantic conventions](https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/gen-ai-spans/), `request.model` is the model requested and `response.model` is the model that responded. When the same model handles the request, only the level of specificity should differ — not the format (`.` vs `-`).

Either normalize `request.model` to the API identifier format, or have `response.model` echo back the alias.

## Repro

Collect OTLP from Copilot Chat (via `github.copilot.advanced.debug.collectOTel`) with model set to Claude Opus 4.6. Compare `gen_ai.request.model` vs `gen_ai.response.model` on any `chat` span.

title: BulkTextEdit applies edit although text document version has advanced.

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
For a video showing this see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode-languageserver-node/issues/1756

Although the workspace edit is created using an LSP server the actual problem that this happens is in `BulkTextEdit#apply` (see https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/b8b5144cc6d926aa50402ca2511f3068a6bc7c54/src/vs/workbench/contrib/bulkEdit/browser/bulkTextEdits.ts#L293). 

The method itself is async. It first validates the edit for correct version and the asynchronously creates the edit task, which gives the editor the possibility to accept and process keystrokes.

Easy fix would IMO be to two the two statements so that the code looks like this

```
		const tasks = await this._createEditsTasks();
		this._validateBeforePrepare();
```

body:

<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->
- VS Code Version: 1.118.1 (Universal)
- OS Version: Darwin arm64 25.3.0

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Make sure `editor.inlineSuggest.minShowDelay` is the default (zero) and Copilot is enabled.
2. Add a snippet for the language you're working on (e.g. PHP)
3. Start typing a snippet name. Copilot shows an inline suggestion, but the dropdown to pick completions does not show.
4. Set `editor.inlineSuggest.minShowDelay` to 1000 and try again. The completions show.

This is similar to https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/311596, but I thought it made sense to add a separate issue since this impacts snippets too and can be tested without any extensions.

I noticed this within the last week or two, so it's probably something in 1.117 or 1.118.

title: Agents Window chat input history is shared across sessions

labels.name: feature-request
labels.description: Request for new features or functionality

body:
When using the Agents Window with multiple sessions open, chat input history is shared across sessions.

## Repro

1. Open the Agents Window.
2. Open or create two different sessions.
3. Send a few prompts in the first session.
4. Switch to the second session and send different prompts there.
5. Press Up Arrow in the chat input to navigate previous prompts.

## Actual

The input history can include prompts from other sessions. For example, pressing Up Arrow in session B can show prompts that were sent in session A.

## Expected

Chat input history should be scoped to the current session. Pressing Up/Down should only navigate prompts submitted in that same session.

## Demo

I have a video below showing the shared-history behavior. (The sessions were in two different folders)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/49f2c394-5709-4088-8e0f-ef581c62254c

title: Custom Endpoint BYOK provider sends wrong auth header for Azure OpenAI-compatible endpoints behind APIM

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
<!-- ⚠️⚠️ Do Not Delete This! bug_report_template ⚠️⚠️ -->
<!-- Please read our Rules of Conduct: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/ -->
<!-- 🕮 Read our guide about submitting issues: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/wiki/Submitting-Bugs-and-Suggestions -->
<!-- 🔎 Search existing issues to avoid creating duplicates. -->
<!-- 🧪 Test using the latest Insiders build to see if your issue has already been fixed: https://code.visualstudio.com/insiders/ -->
<!-- 💡 Instead of creating your report here, use 'Report Issue' from the 'Help' menu in VS Code to pre-fill useful information. -->
<!-- 🔧 Launch with `code --disable-extensions` to check. -->
Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: No

<!-- 🪓 If you answered No above, use 'Help: Start Extension Bisect' from Command Palette to try to identify the cause. -->
<!-- 📣 Issues caused by an extension need to be reported directly to the extension publisher. The 'Help > Report Issue' dialog can assist with this. -->

- Copilot Chat Extension Version: Bundled with VS Code Insiders 1.122.0-insider
- VS Code Version: 1.122.0-insider
- Commit: b51c2a1a37574dec35d0426b2ee9c3538c9774f4
- OS Version: Windows_NT x64 10.0.26200
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): BYOK language model provider, Custom Endpoint (`customendpoint`), Responses API
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Custom Endpoint model using `apiType: "responses"`
- Logs: HTTP traces show direct Azure OpenAI/Foundry endpoint receives `api-key`, while APIM endpoint receives `Authorization: Bearer`

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Configure a Custom Endpoint BYOK model with `vendor: "customendpoint"` and `apiType: "responses"`.

   Example direct Azure OpenAI/Foundry-style endpoint:

   ```json
   [
     {
       "name": "Foundry",
       "vendor": "customendpoint",
       "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.example}",
       "apiType": "responses",
       "models": [
         {
           "id": "gpt-5.3-codex",
           "name": "gpt-5.3-codex-foundry",
           "url": "https://example.openai.azure.com/openai/v1",
           "toolCalling": true,
           "vision": true,
           "maxInputTokens": 200000,
           "maxOutputTokens": 64000
         }
       ]
     }
   ]
   ```

2. Send a chat request.

   Actual result: the request is sent with:

   ```http
   api-key: <key>
   ```

   This works for the direct Azure OpenAI endpoint.

3. Configure the same model/provider/API key/API type, but route through an Azure API Management endpoint in front of the same Azure OpenAI-compatible service.

   ```json
   [
     {
       "name": "Foundry",
       "vendor": "customendpoint",
       "apiKey": "${input:chat.lm.secret.example}",
       "apiType": "responses",
       "models": [
         {
           "id": "gpt-5.3-codex",
           "name": "gpt-5.3-codex-apim",
           "url": "https://example.azure-api.net/openai/v1",
           "toolCalling": true,
           "vision": true,
           "maxInputTokens": 200000,
           "maxOutputTokens": 64000
         }
       ]
     }
   ]
   ```

4. Send a chat request.

   Actual result: the request is sent with:

   ```http
   Authorization: Bearer <key>
   ```

   The APIM endpoint expects the same Azure OpenAI-style `api-key` header, so the request fails with `401`.

Expected result:

The Custom Endpoint provider should provide a way to explicitly configure the auth header style for the API key, or should otherwise not infer auth behavior solely from whether the URL contains `openai.azure`.

Why this appears to happen:

The current implementation appears to choose the auth header based on a substring check of the resolved model URL:

```ts
if (this.useMessagesApi) {
	headers['x-api-key'] = this._apiKey;
	headers['anthropic-version'] = '2023-06-01';
	Object.assign(headers, this.getAnthropicBetaHeader());
} else if (this._modelUrl.includes('openai.azure')) {
	headers['api-key'] = this._apiKey;
} else {
	headers['Authorization'] = `Bearer ${this._apiKey}`;
}
```

Source:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/vscode-node/customEndpointProvider.ts

That means a direct Azure OpenAI endpoint such as `*.openai.azure.com` gets `api-key`, but the same endpoint behind APIM, a gateway, a private endpoint, or a vanity domain falls through to `Authorization: Bearer`.

There also does not appear to be a viable `requestHeaders` workaround, because `api-key` and `authorization` are reserved and ignored by header sanitization:

```ts
'api-key',
'authorization',
'content-type',
```

Source:
https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/extensions/copilot/src/extension/byok/node/openAIEndpoint.ts

Suggested fix:

Add an explicit Custom Endpoint configuration option for auth/header mode, for example one of:

```json
"authHeader": "api-key"
```

or

```json
"apiKeyHeaderName": "api-key"
```

or a more general enum:

```json
"authType": "api-key"
```

where supported values could include `api-key`, `bearer`, and `x-api-key`.

This would make Custom Endpoint usable with APIM, gateways, reverse proxies, private endpoints, and enterprise vanity domains where the public hostname is not enough to infer the backend API authentication contract.

> [!NOTE]
> Codex does this nicely with their custom model provider support, by offering the `http_headers` and `env_http_headers` config properties: https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-advanced#custom-model-providers

Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

- VS Code Version: 1.115.0 (41dd792b5e652393e7787322889ed5fdc58bd75b)
- OS Version: Windows 10 Home China 26200

Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code on Windows.
2. Create two integrated terminals in the editor area (not in the panel). In my case both use Git Bash.
3. Make sure they are separate shell sessions, for example `echo $$` returns different PIDs in terminal A and terminal B.
4. Leave both terminals idle.
5. From the Explorer, drag a file into terminal A while holding Shift so VS Code inserts the file path into the terminal instead of opening the file in the editor.
6. Observe that terminal B also receives the same file path even though the file was only dropped on terminal A.

Expected:
Only terminal A should receive the dropped file path.

Actual:
Both terminal editor instances receive the same file path.

Notes:
- Reproduces with `code --disable-extensions`.
- This is specifically about terminals opened in the editor area.
- If I do not hold Shift, the file opens in the editor, which is expected.
- The two terminals are not the same shell session. Example PIDs from `echo $$`: A=8554, B=8599.

title: Cross-file inline edit list never disposed; handleListEndOfLifetime not fired

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

labels.name: NES
labels.description: Issues related to the next edit suggestions feature

labels.name: NES-ux
labels.description: UI / UX issues related to the next edit suggestions feature

body:
## Summary

When the user accepts an inline edit whose target is a different document, the producing `InlineSuggestionList` is never disposed. As a consequence, the extension-host `InlineCompletionItemProvider.handleListEndOfLifetime` callback never fires for cross-file inline edits, and any end-of-life telemetry the extension sends from that callback is silently dropped.

This was caught while investigating missing `provideInlineEdit` telemetry for cross-file NES in the Copilot Chat extension.

## Steps to reproduce

0. Edit `example.ts` such that it becomes a recently-edited file such that it's included in NES context
1. Go to `math.ts` and change a symbol imported in `example.ts` , eg change `add` to `plus` 
2. You should get a cross-file NES suggesting to rename `add` usage to `plus` in `example.ts`
3. :bug: Observe you don't get telemetry sent for this opportunity 

Expected: the extension provider receives `handleListEndOfLifetime` for the cross-file list.
Actual: it never fires.

## Diagnosis

`InlineCompletionsModel.transplantCompletion` calls `item.addRef()` with no matching `removeRef`:

```ts
public transplantCompletion(item: InlineSuggestionItem): void {
    item.addRef();                                  // unpaired
    transaction(tx => {
        this._source.seedWithCompletion(item, tx);  // already takes its own ref
        ...
    });
}
```

`seedWithCompletion` constructs a `new InlineCompletionsState([item], …)` whose ctor already calls `addRef` on every item it holds and pairs it with `removeRef` in its dispose. The explicit `item.addRef()` is therefore redundant and leaks one ref on every cross-file accept, pinning the `InlineSuggestionList` at `refCount ≥ 1` forever. `disposeInlineCompletions` only runs when refCount hits 0, so `MainThreadLanguageFeatures.disposeInlineCompletions` → `extHostLanguageFeatures.disposeCompletions` → `provider.handleListEndOfLifetime` never runs.

I confirmed by adding temporary instrumentation to `InlineSuggestionList` (per-instance id, captured `addRef` stacks, a `globalThis.__inlineCompletionListDump()` hook, a `setTimeout` leak watchdog). The dump showed one leaked list per cross-file accept with an unpaired `addRef` stack pointing at `transplantCompletion`.

`transplantCompletion` was introduced in #290036 (commit `37ac613`); the unpaired addRef has been there since that commit.

## Fix

Drop the redundant `item.addRef()` in `transplantCompletion` and add a cheap always-on invariant (`onUnexpectedError` if refCount goes negative) to catch the inverse mistake.

Fix in PR #318340.

title: Chronicle cloud sync: circuit breaker never recovers within a session

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

The chronicle cloud sync circuit breaker transitions to an open (tripped) state after repeated failures, but never transitions back to a half-open or closed state within the same VS Code session.

Once the breaker trips, cloud sync is effectively dead until the user restarts VS Code. There is no periodic retry or half-open probe to detect when the transient issue has resolved.

## Expected behavior

The circuit breaker should implement a standard half-open state:
- After a cooldown period (e.g., 5 minutes), attempt a single probe request
- If the probe succeeds, transition back to closed and resume normal sync
- If the probe fails, remain open and schedule the next probe with exponential backoff

## Current behavior

- Circuit breaker opens after repeated failures (observed: ~17 consecutive failures before trip)
- Stays permanently open for the remainder of the session
- User must restart VS Code to get cloud sync working again
- No telemetry or notification informs the user that sync has stopped

## Impact

Users in long-running sessions (common for remote/SSH developers) lose cloud sync for hours or days without realizing it. Their session history stops syncing silently.

## Suggested fix

1. Add a half-open state with periodic retry (e.g., exponential backoff starting at 5min, capped at 30min)
2. Emit telemetry when the breaker recovers (closed transition) to validate the fix
3. Consider a status bar indicator or notification when sync is degraded

title: Chronicle cloud sync: createCloudSession should gracefully handle policy-blocked users

labels.name: bug
labels.description: Issue identified by VS Code Team member as probable bug

labels.name: verified
labels.description: Verification succeeded

labels.name: insiders-released
labels.description: Patch has been released in VS Code Insiders

body:
## Problem

When enterprise policies block cloud session creation, the chronicle cloud sync repeatedly attempts to create sessions and fails. There is no caching of the policy-blocked state, so the same request is retried on every session start, generating unnecessary failures and eventually tripping the circuit breaker.

## Current behavior

1. User starts a chat session
2. Chronicle attempts createCloudSession
3. Server returns a policy-blocked error
4. Next session start: same thing repeats
5. After enough failures, circuit breaker trips (see #317330)
6. All cloud sync stops — including for users who might have mixed repos (some allowed, some not)

## Expected behavior

1. On first policy_blocked response, cache the result with a TTL (e.g., 1 hour or until next window reload)
2. Skip createCloudSession attempts for that workspace/org during the TTL
3. Do not count policy_blocked as a circuit breaker failure (it is an expected policy response, not a transient error)
4. Optionally surface a one-time notification: cloud sync is not available for this workspace due to organization policy

## Impact

As chronicle rolls out more broadly, enterprise users with restrictive policies will see repeated silent failures. The current design conflates policy enforcement with infrastructure errors, causing the circuit breaker to trip unnecessarily.

## Suggested fix

1. Distinguish policy_blocked from transient errors in the response handling
2. Cache the blocked state per-workspace with TTL
3. Exclude policy responses from the circuit breaker failure counter
4. Add a pre-flight check or early-exit before attempting createCloudSession when cached as blocked

title: Agents view: session resume fails with "Error during execution" when no git repo
body:
## Bug Description

When resuming a CLI-created Claude Code session from the Agents view, the session fails with:

```
Error: Error during execution
```

The underlying SDK error is:
```
No conversation found with session ID: <uuid>
```

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Create a Claude Code session via CLI in a directory without a git repository (e.g., `C:\Users\username`)
2. Open VS Code Insiders (no workspace open)
3. Open the Agents view — the CLI session appears in the session list
4. Click on the session and send a message (e.g., "hi")
5. **Result**: "Error: Error during execution" is displayed

## Root Cause

When the session has no associated git repository, `copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts` creates a fallback workspace folder with URI `unknown:///` ([line 1266](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/sessions/contrib/providers/copilotChatSessions/browser/copilotChatSessionsProvider.ts#L1266)). `WorkspaceFolderManagementContribution` then registers this as a real workspace folder.

In the Copilot extension, `getFolderInfoForSession()` sees one workspace folder and uses its `fsPath` as the Claude SDK `cwd`. On Windows, `unknown:///` resolves to `fsPath = "\"`. The SDK uses this invalid `cwd` to locate the session file on disk, looking in `~/.claude/projects/\-/` instead of the correct project directory.

## Environment

- VS Code Insiders 1.122.0
- Windows 11
- Claude Code CLI sessions created via Agent Maestro proxy

## Fix

Filter `getWorkspaceFolders()` to only include `file://` scheme URIs in `getFolderInfoForSession()`, so virtual/unknown workspace folders are excluded. See PR.

body:
Type: Bug Since the most recent update, I am unable to get IntelliSense without having Inline Suggestions disabled. I looked inside the settings and tried tweaking all sorts of values and I was unable to get the behavior that existed prior. I can confirm disabling Inline Suggestions completely fixes this broken. I found there are 2 new settings for the copilot modal but I cannot enable Next Edit suggestions without "Ghost text suggestions" enabled. (Which I believe is the setting that causes this issue.) I tried disabling all extensions and this issue has persisted for me. 1. Enable Inline Suggestions 2. No more IntelliSense I am not able to enable inline suggestions without ghosting text suggestions. (Next edit suggestions button is greyed out.) My copilot usage is at 100% however this has still worked before. --- EDIT 1: I was able to resolve this issue by pasting in specific settings ``` { "editor.quickSuggestions": { "other": true, "comments": false, "strings": false, }, "editor.suggestOnTriggerCharacters": true, "editor.inlineSuggest.enabled": true, "editor.suggest.showWords": true, "editor.suggest.preview": true, "github.copilot.nextEditSuggestions.enabled": true, } ``` I contacted my other friends regarding this, and the same thing happened to them also, with the same fix (changing the settings). Important to mention, that to one this has happened 3 weeks ago, therefore it is most likely not an issue with the most recent release but something else. --- EDIT 2: I believe this could be the reason this behavior happens: No suggestions are probably not the intended effect.
